recipes.title: Loco moco
steps: Combine ground beef, bread crumbs, 1 egg, Worcestershire sauce, chopped onion, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Form into 4 patties.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook patties for 4 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through. Set aside.
In the same skillet, melt butter and sauté mushrooms until browned. Sprinkle with flour and stir to coat.
Add soy sauce to beef broth, then slowly pour into the mushroom mixture while stirring. Simmer for 5-10 minutes until gravy thickens.
Fry 4 eggs sunny-side up in a separate pan.
Assemble: scoop rice onto plates, place a burger patty on top, ladle gravy over, and finish with a fried egg. Garnish with green onions.

recipes.title: Garlicky Chicken With Lemon-Anchovy Sauce
ingredients: 1¼ pounds boneless, skinless chicken thighs (4 to 5 thighs)
steps: Heat oven to 350°F. Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Mince one garlic clove and reserve. In an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat, warm the olive oil, then add the five smashed garlic cloves, anchovies, capers, and chile flakes. Cook 3 to 5 minutes, stirring to break up the anchovies, until the garlic browns and the anchovies dissolve.
Add the chicken thighs and sear until well browned on one side, 5 to 7 minutes. Flip the thighs, transfer the skillet to the oven, and roast 5 to 10 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.

Transfer chicken to a plate. Return the skillet to the stove, add the reserved minced garlic and juice from half the lemon, and cook about 30 seconds while scraping up browned bits. Return the chicken to the pan and cook in the sauce for another 15 to 30 seconds. Transfer to a platter, squeeze the remaining lemon half over the top, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve.

recipes.title: Beef and Guiness Stew
ingredients: 600g chopped Maris piper potatoes (I didnt bother peeling) I quartered the potatoes to even sized pieces. (You could leave out the potatoes and serve with mash separately)
steps: preheat oven to 180 celcius
in a large skillet sweat your veg in oil for 3-4mins before adding your beef & flour and browning on all sides (about 6more mins)
add all your remaining ingredients and stir and cover.
bake for at least 3 hours (stir occasionally)

recipes.title: Honey lime chicken bowl
steps: tsp salt
/2 tsp black pepper
tsp paprika
tsp onion powder
/2 tsp dried oregano For the Bowls:
cup fire-roasted corn (you can use frozen and thawed or canned)
cucumber, thinly sliced (use raw or pickled, per preference)
/2 red onion, thinly sliced (use raw or pickled, per preference) Lime wedges, for serving Fresh cilantro, chopped (optional)
Marinate the Chicken:

In a bowl, whisk together liquid aminos, honey, lime juice, and minced garlic. Add the chicken thighs to the marinade, making sure they are fully coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours for more flavor.
Cook the Chicken:
Preheat a skillet or grill pan over medium heat.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and pat it dry with paper towels. Discard the excess marinade.
In a small bowl, mix the salt, pepper, paprika, onion powder, and oregano. Season both sides of the chicken thighs with the seasoning mixture.
Add a little olive oil to the pan, then cook the chicken thighs for 6-7 minutes per side, or until the internal temperature reaches 165F (74C) and they are golden and caramelized.
Let the chicken rest for a few minutes.
Prepare the Bowls:
Divide the cooked rice among four bowls.
Top with fire-roasted corn, sliced cucumbers, and red onions (raw or pickled as preferred).
Add the sliced honey lime chicken thighs on top of the rice and veggies.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with lime wedges on the side for an extra zesty kick.

recipes.title: Blueberry cream pancake
steps: combine all ingredients in a blender
Grease a pan with butter and add the mix with some frozen blueberries
Cook each side on medium low heat until golden brown
Mix 1/2 cup greek yogurt (11G) protein with 1 tsp peanut butter and defrost some frozen blueberries
Stack 4 pancakes (38G) with yogurt mix (11G) in between and top with blueberries and optional 1 tsp maple syrup One stack of 4 pancakes with the entire yogurt mix gives you 49G PROTEIN!!!!

recipes.title: Finish Fish soup
ingredients: 500g salmon fillet, skinless, boneless, cut into 2-3 cm cubes
1 small leek, thinly sliced (white and light green parts)
3 tbsp fresh dill, chopped, plus extra for garnish
steps: Step 1: In a large pot, melt butter over medium heat. Add sliced leek and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Step 2: Add diced carrots and potatoes to the pot. Pour in the fish stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until potatoes are almost tender.
Step 3: Gently add the salmon cubes to the simmering soup. Cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily.
Step 4: Stir in the heavy cream. Heat the soup through for 2-3 minutes, but do not bring it to a rolling boil after adding the cream.
Step 5: Season with salt and white pepper. Stir in the chopped fresh dill. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Step 6: Ladle into bowls and garnish with extra fresh dill before serving.

recipes.title: Potatoe mushrooms cups
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).

Wash and scrub potatoes. Cut each potato in half lengthwise. Using a spoon, carefully scoop out the center of each potato half, leaving a 1/4-inch thick shell to form a "cup". Chop the scooped-out potato flesh and set aside.
Place the potato cups in a pot of salted boiling water and par-boil for 5-7 minutes, or until slightly tender but still firm. Drain well and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.

Add the chopped mushrooms and the reserved chopped potato flesh to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until mushrooms release their liquid and it evaporates, and potatoes are tender, about 8-10 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and dried thyme, cook for another minute until fragrant.
Remove from heat. Stir in heavy cream (if using), salt, and pepper to taste.
Place the par-boiled potato cups on a baking sheet. Fill each potato cup generously with the mushroom mixture.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until potatoes are tender and the filling is heated through. If using cheese, sprinkle it over the cups during the last 5 minutes of baking until melted and bubbly.
Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

recipes.title: Beef stew
ingredients: 2 lbs beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 tsp salt, plus more to taste
1/2 tsp black pepper, plus more to taste
3 carrots, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
2 large potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
steps: Step 1: Pat the beef cubes dry with paper towels. In a shallow dish, combine the flour, 1 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp pepper. Dredge the beef cubes in the flour mixture, shaking off any excess.

Step 2: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Brown the beef in batches on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per batch. Remove the browned beef and set aside.
Step 3: Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté over medium heat for 5-7 minutes, until the vegetables begin to soften.
Step 4: Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Step 5: If using, pour in the red wine and scrape the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon to deglaze, loosening any browned bits. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the wine has reduced slightly.
Step 6: Return the browned beef to the pot. Pour in the beef broth, add the bay leaf, dried thyme, and Worcestershire sauce (if using). Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Step 7: Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 1 hour 30 minutes to 2 hours, or until the beef is very tender.
Step 8: Add the cubed potatoes to the stew. Continue to simmer, covered, for another 30-45 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Step 9: Remove the bay leaf. Taste the stew and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed. Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.

recipes.title: Hassle back potato
ingredients: 4 medium Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes
2 tbsp olive oil or melted butter
1 tbsp fresh rosemary or thyme, chopped (optional)
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Step 2: Wash and scrub the potatoes thoroughly. Pat them dry.

Step 3: Place each potato on a cutting board. To prevent cutting all the way through, place a chopstick or the handle of a wooden spoon on either side of the potato. Make thin, even slices about 1/8-inch apart, cutting down until your knife hits the chopsticks/spoon, leaving the bottom intact.
Step 4: Place the sliced potatoes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or foil.
Step 5: In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil (or melted butter), salt, black pepper, and garlic powder (if using).
Step 6: Brush the oil mixture generously over the top and into the slices of each potato. If using fresh herbs, sprinkle them over the potatoes now.

Step 7: Bake for 45-60 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork and the edges are crispy and golden brown. For extra crispiness, you can increase the oven temperature to 425°F (220°C) for the last 10-15 minutes.
Step 8: Serve immediately as a side dish.

recipes.title: Salmon Wellington
ingredients: 4 oz (113g) cream cheese, softened (or Boursin garlic & herb cheese)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Step 2: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes. Drain well, pressing out excess water. Let cool slightly.
Step 3: In a small bowl, combine the drained spinach, softened cream cheese, chopped dill, lemon zest, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Step 4: Season salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Step 5: Unroll the puff pastry sheet onto the prepared baking sheet. Cut the pastry in half lengthwise to create two rectangles.
Step 6: On one half of each pastry rectangle, spread half of the spinach mixture, leaving a border around the edges.
Step 7: Place one seasoned salmon fillet on top of the spinach mixture on each pastry half.

Step 8: Fold the other half of the puff pastry over the salmon and spinach, aligning the edges. Crimp the edges firmly with a fork to seal. You can also make decorative cuts on top of the pastry if desired.
Step 9: Brush the tops and sides of the pastry parcels with the beaten egg wash.
Step 10: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed, and the salmon is cooked through (internal temperature of 145°F / 63°C).
Step 11: Let rest for a few minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Fall sangria
ingredients: 1 (750ml) bottle dry red wine (e.g., Pinot Noir, Merlot)
1 large apple (e.g., Honeycrisp, Fuji), cored and sliced
1 large pear (e.g., Bosc, Anjou), cored and sliced
2 tablespoons maple syrup or brown sugar (optional, to taste)
Sparkling water or club soda (for serving, optional)
steps: Step 1: In a large pitcher or punch bowl, combine the red wine, apple cider, and brandy.
Step 2: Add the sliced orange, apple, pear, cinnamon sticks, and cranberries to the pitcher.
Step 3: Stir gently to combine all ingredients. If desired, add maple syrup or brown sugar to sweeten to your preference.
Step 4: Cover the pitcher and refrigerate for at least 2-4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to meld and the fruit to infuse the sangria.

Step 5: When ready to serve, stir the sangria again. Serve over ice, garnished with extra fresh fruit if desired. Top with a splash of sparkling water or club soda for a lighter, effervescent drink, if preferred.

recipes.title: Three cup chicken
ingredients: 1.5 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs or drumsticks, chopped into 2-inch pieces
1 large knob ginger, peeled and sliced (about 2-3 inches)
1 whole head garlic, cloves peeled and smashed
steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, combine the soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, and sugar. Set aside.
Step 2: Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and brown them on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
Step 3: Add the sliced ginger, smashed garlic, and dried red chilies (if using) to the same pan. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Step 4: Return the browned chicken to the pan. Pour in the prepared sauce mixture and the sesame oil. Bring to a simmer.
Step 5: Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the pan, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Step 6: Uncover the pan, increase the heat to medium-high, and cook for another 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has reduced and thickened slightly, coating the chicken.
Step 7: Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the fresh Thai basil leaves until just wilted.
Step 8: Serve hot, preferably with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Beef short ribs parpadelle
ingredients: 1.5 cups dry red wine (e.g., Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot)
1 tsp salt, plus more to taste
1/2 tsp black pepper, plus more to taste
Freshly grated Parmesan cheese, for serving
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Season the short ribs generously with salt and pepper.

Step 2: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium-high heat. Sear the short ribs on all sides until deeply browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove ribs from the pot and set aside.

Step 3: Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté over medium heat until softened, about 8-10 minutes. Add the minced garlic and tomato paste, cooking for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Step 4: Pour in the red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Bring to a simmer and cook until the wine has reduced by about half, 5-7 minutes.
Step 5: Stir in the beef broth, crushed tomatoes, bay leaves, and thyme sprigs. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.
Step 6: Return the seared short ribs to the pot, ensuring they are mostly submerged in the liquid. Cover the Dutch oven tightly with a lid.
Step 7: Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise for 2.5 to 3 hours, or until the short ribs are fork-tender and easily pull away from the bone.
Step 8: Carefully remove the short ribs from the pot and place them on a cutting board. Discard the bay leaves and thyme sprigs from the sauce.
Step 9: Skim any excess fat from the surface of the sauce. If the sauce is too thin, simmer it on the stovetop over medium-high heat until it reaches your desired consistency.
Step 10: Once the ribs are cool enough to handle, shred the meat from the bones using two forks. Discard the bones and any large pieces of fat or gristle. Stir the shredded beef into the sauce.
Step 11: Cook the parpadelle pasta according to package directions in a large pot of salted boiling water until al dente. Drain well.
Step 12: Add the drained parpadelle directly to the pot with the short rib ragu, tossing to combine.
Step 13: Serve immediately, garnished with freshly grated Parmesan cheese and chopped fresh parsley.

recipes.title: Red wine braised short ribs
steps: Pat the short ribs dry, coat in olive oil and season generously on all sides with salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Get a large greased skillet going over medium high heat and sear the short ribs on all sides until you get a nice crust. Once done, set aside.

Get a dutch oven or any large oven-safe pan/pot going over medium heat. Add in some olive followed by your celery, carrots, onions and garlic. Season with salt and pepper then mix. Let saute for a few minutes then clear a spot in the middle and add in the tomato paste. Mix that in tell everything is well combined then place your short ribs on top. Pour in the wine and beef broth untill the liquid covering the top of the short ribs. Add in the thyme, rosemary and bay leaf. Place the lid on and pop it in the oven at 325 for 2.5-3 hours or until the short ribs are fall apart tender.
Once the short ribs are done, turn off the oven & leave them in there while you make the garlic-parm potato puree.

recipes.title: Potato side dish
ingredients: 2 lbs small potatoes (such as Yukon Gold or red potatoes)
2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped (optional, for garnish)
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Step 2: Wash and cut potatoes into 1-inch pieces. Pat them thoroughly dry with a paper towel.
Step 3: In a large bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, dried rosemary, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Step 4: Add the cut potatoes to the bowl and toss until they are evenly coated with the oil and herb mixture.
Step 5: Spread the potatoes in a single layer on a large baking sheet.
Step 6: Roast for 35-45 minutes, or until golden brown and tender, flipping the potatoes halfway through cooking.
Step 7: Remove from oven, garnish with fresh parsley if desired, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Baked sweet potatoes
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Wash the sweet potatoes thoroughly and pat dry.
Prick each sweet potato several times with a fork.
Rub sweet potatoes with olive oil (if using), then sprinkle evenly with salt and pepper.
Place sweet potatoes directly on the oven rack or on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 45-60 minutes, or until very tender when pierced with a fork.
Carefully remove from oven, slice lengthwise, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Crispy chicken herby rice
steps: Step 1: Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towels. Season both sides with 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper, and paprika.
Step 2: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Place chicken thighs skin-side down and cook for 8-10 minutes, until skin is deeply golden and crispy.

Step 3: Flip chicken and cook for another 5-7 minutes, or until cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F/74°C). If thighs are very thick, you can transfer the skillet to a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 5-10 minutes to finish cooking. Remove chicken from skillet and set aside to rest.

Step 4: While chicken cooks, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute more until fragrant.
Step 5: Add rinsed basmati rice to the saucepan and stir for 1-2 minutes to lightly toast.
Step 6: Pour in chicken broth, add remaining 1/2 tsp salt and 1/4 tsp black pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed.
Step 7: Remove rice from heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Step 8: Fluff the rice with a fork. Stir in chopped parsley, dill, chives, unsalted butter, and lemon zest and juice (if using).
Step 9: Serve the crispy chicken thighs over or alongside the herby rice.

recipes.title: Roasted garlic Parmesan cabbage
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper for easier cleanup.
Step 2: Remove any loose outer leaves from the cabbage. Cut the cabbage into 1-inch thick wedges or slices, keeping the core intact on each piece to hold it together.
Step 3: In a large bowl, combine the olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Add the cabbage wedges and toss gently to coat evenly.
Step 4: Arrange the cabbage in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. If necessary, use two baking sheets.
Step 5: Roast for 20-25 minutes, flipping the cabbage halfway through, until it is tender-crisp and slightly browned at the edges.
Step 6: Remove the baking sheet from the oven. Sprinkle the grated Parmesan cheese evenly over the roasted cabbage.
Step 7: Return to the oven and continue to roast for another 5-7 minutes, or until the Parmesan cheese is melted and lightly golden brown.
Step 8: Serve hot as a side dish.

recipes.title: One pan roasted lemon chicken
ingredients: 1.5-2 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or a mix)
1.5 lbs baby potatoes, halved or quartered
2 lemons, one sliced into rounds, one quartered
1 tbsp fresh rosemary, chopped (or 1 tsp dried)
1 tbsp fresh thyme, chopped (or 1 tsp dried)
1/2 tsp black pepper, or to taste
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Step 2: Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper.
Step 3: On a large baking sheet, combine the halved or quartered potatoes, onion wedges, lemon quarters, and smashed garlic. Drizzle with olive oil.
Step 4: Add the seasoned chicken pieces to the baking sheet. Sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary and thyme.

Step 5: Toss everything together on the baking sheet until the chicken and vegetables are well coated with oil and herbs. Arrange in a single layer, placing the lemon slices on top of or around the chicken.

Step 6: Roast for 35-45 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F/74°C) and the vegetables are tender and lightly browned. For extra crispiness, you can broil for the last 2-3 minutes.
Step 7: Remove from the oven and let rest for a few minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Baked lemon chicken
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towels.
Step 2: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, minced garlic, dried oregano, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper. Squeeze the juice of one lemon into the mixture.
Step 3: Add the chicken thighs to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the lemon-herb mixture. Ensure all pieces are well covered.
Step 4: Arrange the chicken thighs in a single layer in a large baking dish. Slice the remaining lemon into thin rounds and tuck them in between and on top of the chicken pieces.
Step 5: If using, pour the chicken broth into the bottom of the baking dish. This helps keep the chicken moist.
Step 6: Bake for 35-45 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F / 74°C) and the skin is golden brown and crispy.
Step 7: Remove from oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Honey garlic chicken
ingredients: 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
Salt and black pepper to taste
2 tbsp chopped green onions (for garnish, optional)
steps: Step 1: In a medium bowl, whisk together honey, soy sauce, minced garlic, rice vinegar, and sesame oil. Set aside.
Step 2: Season chicken pieces with salt and black pepper.
Step 3: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from skillet and set aside.
Step 4: In a small bowl, whisk cornstarch with water to create a slurry.
Step 5: Pour the honey garlic sauce (from Step 1) into the skillet. Bring to a simmer. Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 1-2 minutes.
Step 6: Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and toss to coat evenly with the sauce.
Step 7: Garnish with sesame seeds and chopped green onions, if desired. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Octopus pasta
steps: Step 1: If using frozen octopus, thaw it completely. Clean the octopus thoroughly if fresh.

Step 2: In a large pot, bring plenty of water to a boil. Dip the octopus tentacles in and out of the boiling water 3-4 times to help them curl. Submerge the octopus, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 60-90 minutes, or until very tender when pierced with a fork. Drain and let cool slightly.
Step 3: Once cool enough to handle, cut the octopus into bite-sized pieces.

Step 4: In a large skillet or pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Step 5: If using, pour in the white wine and let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until mostly evaporated.

Step 6: Stir in the crushed tomatoes and the cooked octopus pieces. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 20-30 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

Step 7: While the sauce simmers, cook the pasta according to package directions in a separate pot of generously salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve about 1/2 cup of the pasta cooking water before draining.
Step 8: Add the drained pasta to the octopus sauce. Toss well to combine, adding a little reserved pasta water if the sauce is too thick.
Step 9: Stir in the fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Pear puff pastry
steps: cinnamon sticks For the filling:
g chopped walnuts
g honey cinnamon Zest of orange
Prepare a spiced syrup by adding water, sugar, vanilla, lemon zest, cinnamon, and star anise to a pot. Bring to a boil.

Peel the pears, cut them in half, remove the cores, and place them in the boiling syrup. Cook for 10-15 minutes (depending on the initial firmness of the fruit). Test with a toothpick; if it goes in and out easily, the pears are done.
Carefully remove the pears and let them drain on paper towels.
Prepare a fine mixture of walnuts, honey, and grated orange zest, then fill the pears.

Cut the puff pastry into 6 rectangles and place a pear in the center of each. Make a light cut around the shape of the pear and brush with the syrup. If desired, add some sugar to make them even crunchier.
Bake at 180C (350F) for about 25 minutes, until cooked and golden brown.
Add a mint leaf and serve with a light dusting of powdered sugar. What other fruit could you make these with?

recipes.title: Shrimp mouchaca
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 cup dry white wine (or chicken broth)
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.

Prepare eggplant: Arrange eggplant slices on paper towels, sprinkle with salt, and let sit for 15-20 minutes to draw out moisture. Pat dry thoroughly. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry eggplant slices in batches until golden brown and tender, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove and set aside on paper towels.

Prepare shrimp filling: In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Sauté chopped onion over medium heat until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute more until fragrant.

Add shrimp to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until they just start to turn pink. Stir in diced tomatoes (undrained), tomato paste, white wine (if using), oregano, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly. Remove from heat.

Prepare Béchamel Sauce: In a medium saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Whisk in flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to form a roux. Gradually whisk in the warm milk, a little at a time, ensuring no lumps form. Continue whisking until the sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5-7 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stir in Parmesan cheese if desired.

Assemble Mouchaca: Spread a thin layer of the shrimp filling on the bottom of the prepared baking dish. Arrange half of the fried eggplant slices over the shrimp filling. Top with half of the remaining shrimp filling.
Pour half of the Béchamel sauce over the shrimp filling. Repeat with the remaining eggplant slices, then the rest of the shrimp filling, and finally top with the remaining Béchamel sauce.
Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and bubbly. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Brie and apple tart
ingredients: 4-6 oz (115-170g) brie cheese, rind on, thinly sliced
1-2 medium apples (e.g., Honeycrisp, Granny Smith), cored and thinly sliced
1 teaspoon fresh thyme, chopped (or 1/2 tsp dried)
1 large egg, beaten (for egg wash, optional)
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

Unroll the puff pastry onto the prepared baking sheet. Score a 1-inch border around the edges of the pastry with a knife, being careful not to cut all the way through. Prick the center area of the pastry with a fork several times.
Arrange the sliced brie cheese evenly over the center of the pastry, within the scored border.
Arrange the apple slices over the brie cheese.
Drizzle honey over the apples and brie. Sprinkle with fresh thyme and a pinch of black pepper, if using.
Brush the scored border of the puff pastry with the beaten egg (egg wash), if using.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed, and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.

recipes.title: Honey butter milk toast
ingredients: 2 slices thick-cut bread (brioche or milk bread recommended)
steps: Step 1: In a shallow dish, whisk together the egg, milk, granulated sugar (if using), and a pinch of salt until well combined.
Step 2: Place the bread slices into the milk mixture, allowing them to soak for 1-2 minutes per side, or until fully saturated but not falling apart.
Step 3: Heat 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter in a non-stick skillet over medium heat until melted and slightly foamy.
Step 4: Carefully place the soaked bread slices into the hot skillet. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and slightly crispy.

Step 5: While the toast is cooking, prepare the honey butter: In a small microwave-safe bowl or saucepan, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter. Stir in the 1 tablespoon of honey until well combined.
Step 6: Transfer the cooked toast to a plate. Drizzle generously with the honey butter and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Creamy coconut noodle soup
steps: Step 1: Heat coconut oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-5 minutes.
Step 2: Add minced garlic, grated ginger, and red curry paste. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Step 3: Pour in vegetable broth and coconut milk. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat and let it bubble gently for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Step 4: Stir in soy sauce and brown sugar (if using). Add sliced red bell pepper and mushrooms. Cook for 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender-crisp.
Step 5: Add rice noodles to the pot. Cook according to package directions, usually 3-5 minutes, until tender.
Step 6: Stir in baby spinach until wilted, about 1 minute.
Step 7: Remove from heat. Stir in lime juice. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

recipes.title: Slow roasted oxtail
steps: Preheat oven to 150°C (300°F).
Season oxtail generously with salt and pepper. If desired, lightly dredge the oxtail pieces in flour.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium-high heat.
Brown oxtail in batches until well-seared on all sides. Remove the browned oxtail and set aside.
Add chopped onions, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté until softened, about 8-10 minutes.
Add minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Pour in the red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Bring to a simmer and reduce by about one-third.
Return the browned oxtail to the pot. Add beef broth, bay leaves, and thyme sprigs. Ensure the oxtail is mostly submerged in the liquid.
Bring the liquid to a gentle simmer, then cover the pot tightly.
Transfer the covered pot to the preheated oven and slow roast for 3-4 hours, or until the meat is fork-tender and easily falls off the bone.
Carefully remove the pot from the oven. Skim any excess fat from the surface of the sauce. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Serve the slow roasted oxtail hot with your preferred accompaniment, such as mashed potatoes, polenta, or crusty bread.

recipes.title: Mochi banana bread
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9x5 inch loaf pan, or line with parchment paper.
Step 2: For the Banana Bread Batter: In a medium bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, baking soda, and salt.
Step 3: In a large bowl, mash the ripe bananas. Add 1/2 cup granulated sugar, brown sugar, vegetable oil, 1 large egg, and vanilla extract. Mix until well combined.
Step 4: Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined. Do not overmix. Fold in chocolate chips if using. Pour this batter into the prepared loaf pan.
Step 5: For the Mochi Batter: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the glutinous rice flour and 1/2 cup granulated sugar.
Step 6: Add 1 cup milk, 1 large egg, and the 2 tbsp melted butter to the mochi flour mixture. Whisk until smooth and lump-free.
Step 7: Carefully pour the mochi batter evenly over the banana bread batter in the loaf pan.

Step 8: Bake for 55-65 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean (it might be slightly sticky from the mochi, but not wet batter). If the top browns too quickly, tent loosely with foil.
Step 9: Let the bread cool in the pan for 15 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely before slicing.

recipes.title: Eggplant involtini
ingredients: 2 large eggplants, about 1.5 to 2 pounds each
1/4 cup olive oil, plus more for brushing
1 teaspoon salt, plus more for salting eggplant
10 ounces frozen chopped spinach, thawed and thoroughly squeezed dry (or 1 pound fresh spinach, wilted and squeezed dry)
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese, plus more for topping
steps: Preheat oven to 375F (190C). Lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Wash and trim the ends of the eggplants. Slice eggplants lengthwise into 1/4-inch thick slices.
Lay eggplant slices in a single layer on paper towels. Sprinkle generously with salt and let sit for 30 minutes to draw out excess moisture.
After 30 minutes, use more paper towels to blot the eggplant slices dry, pressing firmly to remove as much liquid as possible.
Brush both sides of the eggplant slices lightly with olive oil.

Heat a large grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Grill eggplant slices in batches for 2-3 minutes per side, until tender and lightly browned with grill marks. Alternatively, bake on baking sheets at 400F (200C) for 10-15 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender.
In a medium bowl, combine ricotta cheese, squeezed dry spinach, 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese, egg, minced garlic, nutmeg, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper. Mix well.
Spread a thin layer of marinara sauce on the bottom of the prepared baking dish.
Lay an eggplant slice flat. Place about 1-2 tablespoons of the ricotta mixture at one end of the slice. Carefully roll up the eggplant slice from the filled end.
Place the rolled eggplant seam-side down in the baking dish over the marinara sauce. Repeat with remaining eggplant slices and filling.
Pour the remaining marinara sauce over the rolled eggplant involtini.
Sprinkle evenly with shredded mozzarella cheese and additional grated Parmesan cheese.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbly and the cheese is melted and lightly golden brown.
Let stand for 5-10 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh basil leaves.

recipes.title: Stir fry udon
ingredients: 1 lb fresh or frozen udon noodles
1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs, thinly sliced (or tofu/shrimp)
steps: Step 1: Cook udon noodles according to package directions. Drain, rinse with cold water to prevent sticking, and set aside.
Step 2: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, mirin, brown sugar, sesame oil, and if using, cornstarch dissolved in water. Set aside.

Step 3: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken (or protein of choice) and stir-fry until cooked through, about 3-5 minutes. Remove chicken from the wok and set aside.
Step 4: Add onion, bell pepper, and broccoli to the wok. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Step 5: Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Step 6: Return cooked chicken to the wok. Add the cooked udon noodles and pour the prepared sauce over everything.
Step 7: Toss well to coat all ingredients evenly and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and everything is heated through. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Coconut curry ramen
steps: lime

packs ramen noodles Green onions Cilantro Crushed peanuts Vegetable toppings: raw or steamed bell pepper, snow peas, bok choy, mushrooms, etc The full recipe is on my blog if you want to print it out!

recipes.title: Garlic herb cabbage
ingredients: 1 medium head green cabbage, cored and sliced into 1/2-inch strips
1/2 teaspoon dried oregano (or other dried herbs like thyme or an Italian blend)
1/4 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat.
Step 2: Add minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn.
Step 3: Add the sliced cabbage to the skillet. Toss to coat with oil and garlic.
Step 4: Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Cover the skillet and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cabbage begins to soften.
Step 5: Uncover the skillet. Add dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine.
Step 6: Continue to cook, uncovered, for another 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cabbage reaches your desired tenderness (tender-crisp or softer).
Step 7: Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve hot.

recipes.title: Garlic butter clams
ingredients: 2 lbs fresh clams (e.g., Manila or littleneck)
1/2 cup dry white wine (like Sauvignon Blanc or Pinot Grigio)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipes.notes: Ingredients not detected automatically.
recipes.descriptionPreview: 3,667 likes, 80 comments - ayesha_rare on September 18, 2024: "Save this recipe for this classic clams dish to use allll year long!   The perfect weeknight dish with simple ingredients that will keep your friends and family asking for more. Send this to someone you want to try th
steps: Step 1: Clean the clams. Place them in a large bowl of cold water for 20-30 minutes. Scrub the shells clean with a brush. Discard any clams that are open and do not close when tapped.
Step 2: In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the butter over medium heat.
Step 3: Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to brown it.
Step 4: Pour in the white wine and bring it to a simmer.
Step 5: Add the cleaned clams to the pot. Cover tightly with a lid.
Step 6: Cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking the pot occasionally, until all the clams have opened. Discard any clams that do not open.
Step 7: Stir in the fresh parsley and lemon juice. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
Step 8: Serve immediately, optionally with crusty bread for dipping in the delicious broth.

recipes.title: Panera souffls
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly butter 4-6 individual ramekins (4-ounce to 6-ounce size).
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper until well combined and slightly frothy.
Stir in the Parmesan, cheddar, Monterey Jack, and feta cheeses. Add the chopped fresh spinach and mix gently.
Carefully pour the egg mixture evenly into the prepared ramekins, filling each about two-thirds full.
Place the ramekins on a baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the soufflés are puffed, golden brown, and set in the center. A knife inserted into the center should come out clean.
Let cool for a few minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Roasted honey butter hasselback
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).

Step 2: Wash and scrub the potatoes thoroughly. Place each potato between two wooden spoon handles or chopsticks to prevent slicing all the way through. Make thin, even slices about 1/8-inch apart, cutting almost to the bottom but not through.

Step 3: Place the sliced potatoes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle with olive oil and season generously with salt and black pepper, making sure to get some seasoning between the slices.
Step 4: Roast for 30 minutes.
Step 5: While potatoes are roasting, melt the butter in a small saucepan. Stir in the honey, minced garlic, and chopped rosemary (if using).
Step 6: After 30 minutes, remove the potatoes from the oven. Brush the honey butter mixture generously over each potato, trying to get some into the slices.
Step 7: Return to the oven and roast for another 25-35 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork and the edges are crispy and golden brown.
Step 8: Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Yogurt cake
ingredients: ¾ cup (180g) plain whole milk yogurt
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch round cake pan or an 8x8 inch square pan.
Step 2: In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
Step 3: In a large bowl, whisk together the sugar, yogurt, vegetable oil, eggs, vanilla extract, and lemon zest (if using) until well combined and smooth.
Step 4: Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are okay.
Step 5: Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and spread evenly.
Step 6: Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean. The top should be golden brown.
Step 7: Let the cake cool in the pan for 10-15 minutes before inverting it onto a wire rack to cool completely.

recipes.title: Charcuterie cornucopia
ingredients: 4 oz salami, various types (e.g., genoa, soppressata)
4 oz chorizo or other cured sausage
4 oz aged cheddar cheese, sliced or cubed
2 tbsp fig jam or fruit preserve
Fresh rosemary or thyme for garnish (optional)
steps: Step 1: Gather all ingredients and ensure cheeses and meats are at room temperature for optimal flavor (about 15-20 minutes out of the refrigerator).
Step 2: Arrange the cured meats on a large serving board or platter. Fold or roll slices attractively.
Step 3: Place the various cheeses strategically around the board. Cut some into cubes, slice others, and leave wedges whole for visual appeal.
Step 4: Arrange the sliced baguette and assorted crackers in different sections of the board.
Step 5: Fill small bowls with olives, mixed nuts, cornichons, honey, and fig jam. Place these bowls on the board.
Step 6: Scatter fresh grapes, mixed berries, and quartered figs throughout the board, filling in any gaps and adding color.
Step 7: Garnish with fresh rosemary or thyme sprigs if desired.
Step 8: Serve immediately and enjoy.

recipes.title: Swahili grilled chicken
ingredients: 1.5 kg whole chicken, cut into 8 pieces (or 8 chicken pieces like thighs and drumsticks)
1-2 green chilies, minced (optional, adjust to taste)
2 tbsp olive oil or vegetable oil
steps: Step 1: Prepare the chicken by patting the pieces dry with paper towels. Make a few shallow slits in thicker parts of the chicken to help the marinade penetrate.

Step 2: In a large bowl, combine the lemon juice, minced garlic, grated ginger, minced green chilies (if using), ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder, black pepper, salt, and olive oil. Mix well to form the marinade.

Step 3: Add the chicken pieces to the bowl with the marinade, ensuring all pieces are thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best flavor.
Step 4: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (around 375-400°F or 190-200°C). Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.
Step 5: Remove the chicken from the marinade (discard any remaining marinade). Place the chicken pieces on the preheated grill.

Step 6: Grill the chicken for 20-30 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through, the juices run clear, and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). If using, baste with coconut milk during the last 10 minutes of grilling for added moisture and flavor.
Step 7: Remove the grilled chicken from the heat and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro, if desired.

recipes.title: Lemon grass sea bass
ingredients: 2 sea bass fillets - u can use bream too
steps: garlic cloves, minced
2 birds eye chilli, minced
5 tbsp fish sauce
tbsp sugar
tsp msg some hot water (to melt sugar)

wedges of lime chopped coriander *im eyeballing the measurements for dipping sauce but should give u a good base and just adjust to your taste bestie, if its too salty, add water/sugar/lime, if its too sweet add fish sauce/lime etc etc xxx

recipes.title: Garlic cauliflower pasta
ingredients: 1 head cauliflower, cut into small florets
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese, plus more for serving
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Cook pasta according to package directions in a large pot of salted boiling water. Before draining, reserve 1/2 cup of the pasta water. Drain the pasta and set aside.
Step 2: While the pasta cooks, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion (if using) and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Step 3: Add the cauliflower florets to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until tender-crisp and lightly browned, about 8-10 minutes.
Step 4: Add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Step 5: Pour in the vegetable broth and the reserved pasta water. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, allowing the sauce to slightly reduce and flavors to meld.
Step 6: Add the drained pasta to the skillet. Stir in the Parmesan cheese and the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Toss everything together until the pasta is well coated with the sauce.
Step 7: Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh chopped parsley.
Step 8: Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan cheese if desired.

recipes.title: Lemon garlic Parmesan chicken
steps: /4 cup grated parmesan cheese Preheat your oven to 400F or your air fryer to 375F. Rinse and pat dry your chicken pieces. Transfer to a ziploc bag. Top the chicken with the remaining marinade to serve and dip.

recipes.title: Herb crusted salmon
steps: Cup Breadcrumbs Flat Leaf Parsley
Lemon Zest Parmesan Olive Oil Paprika Garlic Granules Onion Salt Olive Oil S&P

recipes.title: French toast crepes
ingredients: Butter or cooking spray for frying
steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, 2 tablespoons of granulated sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Step 2: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together 2 of the large eggs, 1 1/2 cups of milk, and the 2 tablespoons of melted butter.
Step 3: Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking constantly until the batter is smooth and free of lumps. Let the crepe batter rest for 10-15 minutes.

Step 4: Heat a lightly greased 8-inch non-stick pan or crepe maker over medium heat. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the pan, tilting and swirling to coat the bottom evenly. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the edges are set and the bottom is lightly golden. Flip and cook for another 30-60 seconds. Transfer the cooked crepe to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter, stacking the crepes as they are cooked.

Step 5: In a shallow dish (wide enough to dip a crepe), whisk together the remaining 2 large eggs, 1/2 cup of milk, 2 tablespoons of granulated sugar, the vanilla extract, ground cinnamon, and the remaining 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Step 6: Dip each cooked crepe into the French toast batter, ensuring both sides are coated but not overly saturated. Allow any excess batter to drip off.
Step 7: Heat a lightly buttered griddle or large non-stick pan over medium heat. Place the dipped crepes on the griddle, cooking for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Step 8: Serve immediately with your favorite toppings such as maple syrup, fresh berries, or whipped cream.

recipes.title: Octopus pasta
ingredients: 4 tbsp extra virgin olive oil, divided
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Prepare the octopus. In a large pot, bring plenty of water to a rolling boil. Dip the octopus in and out of the boiling water 3-4 times for a few seconds each time (this helps tenderize it). Submerge the octopus completely and simmer gently for 45-60 minutes, or until very tender when pierced with a fork. Remove the octopus from the pot, let it cool slightly, then chop it into bite-sized pieces. Reserve about 1 cup of the cooking liquid.
Step 2: In a large skillet or pot, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
Step 3: Add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Step 4: Pour in the white wine and let it simmer for 2-3 minutes, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.

Step 5: Add the crushed tomatoes and the chopped octopus to the skillet. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. If the sauce becomes too thick, add a ladle or two of the reserved octopus cooking liquid.

Step 6: While the sauce is simmering, cook the pasta according to package directions in a separate pot of generously salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve about 1/2 cup of the pasta cooking water before draining.
Step 7: Add the drained pasta directly to the octopus sauce. Toss well to combine, adding a splash of the reserved pasta water if needed to create a silky sauce that coats the pasta.
Step 8: Stir in the fresh chopped parsley. Drizzle with the remaining 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil.
Step 9: Serve immediately, with an optional squeeze of fresh lemon juice over each serving.

recipes.title: Tiramisu overnight oat
ingredients: 2 tbsp strong brewed coffee or espresso
1-2 tsp maple syrup (or sweetener of choice)
steps: In a jar or container, combine the rolled oats, milk, Greek yogurt, chia seeds, maple syrup, and vanilla extract.
Stir well to ensure all ingredients are thoroughly mixed.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the strong brewed coffee and unsweetened cocoa powder until smooth.
Pour the coffee-cocoa mixture over the oat mixture in the jar. You can gently swirl it in for a marbled effect or mix completely for a uniform flavor.
Cover the jar and refrigerate overnight, or for at least 4-6 hours, until the oats have softened and thickened.
Serve chilled, optionally topped with a dusting of extra cocoa powder or a coffee bean.

recipes.title: coconut chicken bake
ingredients: 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Step 2: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Step 3: Add minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Stir in red curry paste and turmeric powder, cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Step 5: Add chicken pieces and red bell pepper to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until chicken is lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Step 6: Pour in the full-fat coconut milk, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Step 7: Cover the skillet (or transfer to a baking dish if not oven-safe) and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and tender.
Step 8: If using, stir in chopped spinach during the last 5 minutes of baking, or after removing from oven, until wilted.
Step 9: Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

recipes.title: Lemon blueberry cake
ingredients: 1 ½ cups fresh or frozen blueberries
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch round cake pan or line with parchment paper.
Step 2: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Step 3: In a separate medium bowl, cream together the softened butter, eggs, milk, vanilla extract, lemon zest, and 2 tablespoons of lemon juice until well combined.
Step 4: Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing until just combined. Be careful not to overmix.
Step 5: Gently fold in the blueberries. If using frozen blueberries, do not thaw them first.
Step 6: Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and spread evenly.
Step 7: Bake for 35-45 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Step 8: Let the cake cool in the pan for 10-15 minutes before inverting it onto a wire rack to cool completely.

Step 9: While the cake cools, prepare the glaze: In a small bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar and 3-4 tablespoons of fresh lemon juice until smooth. Add more lemon juice if a thinner glaze is desired.
Step 10: Once the cake is completely cool, drizzle the lemon glaze over the top.

recipes.title: Coconut lemon grass lime fish curry
ingredients: 1 lb white fish fillets (e.g., cod, tilapia, or snapper), cut into chunks
2 stalks lemongrass, bruised and cut into 2-inch pieces
1-2 Thai red chilies, finely sliced (adjust to taste)
4-5 kaffir lime leaves (optional, for extra aroma)
steps: Prepare the Fish:

Saute the Aromatics: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the onion and saute for 3-4 minutes until softened. Add sliced chilies, turmeric powder, and ground coriander, stirring to coat the mixture.
Make the Curry Base: Pour in the coconut milk and water (or fish stock). Stir well to combine. Add fish sauce, sugar (if using), kaffir lime leaves, and lime zest. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook the Fish:
Finish with Lime: Remove from heat and stir in lime juice. Adjust seasoning with more fish sauce or lime juice to taste.

Serve: Ladle the curry into bowls and garnish with fresh cilantro, sliced red chili, and lime wedges. Serve hot with steamed jasmine rice or coconut rice. Chefs Tips Fish Options: Use firm white fish like halibut or monkfish to prevent breaking apart in the curry. Extra Heat: Add a teaspoon of red curry paste for more spice".

recipes.title: Banana Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a 9x5-inch loaf pan with parchment.
Whisk together mashed bananas, eggs, brown sugar, melted butter, yogurt, and vanilla until smooth.
In a separate bowl combine flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon.
Fold dry ingredients into the banana mixture until just combined.
Stir in walnuts and chocolate chips, reserving a few for topping.
Transfer batter to the prepared pan, smooth the top, and sprinkle with reserved mix-ins.
Bake 55-60 minutes until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean, then cool on a rack before slicing.

recipes.title: Banana puff pastry
ingredients: 1 large egg, beaten (for egg wash, optional)
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Step 2: Unroll the thawed puff pastry sheet onto a lightly floured surface.
Step 3: Cut the puff pastry sheet into 4-6 equal squares or rectangles.
Step 4: Arrange sliced bananas on one half of each pastry square, leaving a small border. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon (if using).
Step 5: Fold the other half of the pastry over the banana filling, creating a pocket or turnover.
Step 6: Press the edges firmly to seal, then crimp with a fork to ensure a tight seal.
Step 7: If desired, brush the tops of the pastries with the beaten egg wash for a golden finish.
Step 8: Place the pastries on the prepared baking sheet.
Step 9: Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the puff pastry is golden brown and puffed.
Step 10: Let cool slightly before serving.

recipes.title: Cabbage rolls
ingredients: 1.5 lbs ground beef (or a mix of beef and pork)
steps: Core the cabbage. In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook the cabbage for 5-10 minutes until leaves are pliable. Carefully peel off 12-16 large leaves, trimming thick ribs.
Cook rice according to package directions. Let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine ground beef, cooked rice, chopped onion, minced garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, and paprika (if using). Mix well.
Lay a cabbage leaf flat. Place about 1/4 to 1/3 cup of filling near the stem end. Fold in the sides, then roll tightly from the stem end up. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.
In a large Dutch oven or pot, combine crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, water or broth, brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper. Stir to combine.
Place any remaining small cabbage leaves or trimmed ribs at the bottom of the pot. Arrange the cabbage rolls seam-side down on top of the sauce.
Bring the sauce to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until cabbage is very tender and filling is cooked through.
Serve hot.

recipes.title: One pan baked cod
ingredients: 1 lemon, half juiced and half sliced
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, dried dill, salt, and black pepper.
Place the cod fillets on the prepared baking sheet. If using, arrange the asparagus and cherry tomatoes around the cod.
Drizzle the olive oil mixture evenly over the cod and vegetables. Place lemon slices on top of the cod fillets.
Bake for 12-18 minutes, or until the cod is opaque and flakes easily with a fork. Cooking time will vary based on the thickness of the fillets.
Serve immediately with any pan juices.

recipes.title: Butter pasta with sage
ingredients: 250g (about 8 oz) pasta (such as spaghetti, fettuccine, or tagliatelle)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese (for serving, optional)
steps: Step 1: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the pasta and cook according to package directions until al dente. Before draining, reserve about 1/2 cup of the pasta cooking water.

Step 2: While the pasta is cooking, melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the fresh sage leaves and cook gently for 3-5 minutes, or until the butter is fragrant and the sage leaves are slightly crispy but not burnt. The butter should turn a light golden brown (beurre noisette).

Step 3: Drain the pasta and add it directly to the skillet with the sage butter. Toss to coat the pasta thoroughly. If the sauce seems too dry, add a tablespoon or two of the reserved pasta water to create a silky sauce.
Step 4: Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Serve immediately, garnished with grated Parmesan cheese if desired.

recipes.title: Ginger turmeric shot
ingredients: 2 inches fresh ginger, peeled and roughly chopped
1 inch fresh turmeric, peeled and roughly chopped
steps: Peel the fresh ginger and turmeric roots. Roughly chop them into smaller pieces to aid blending.
Add the chopped ginger, turmeric, fresh lemon juice, water, and a pinch of black pepper to a high-speed blender. If desired, add honey or maple syrup for a touch of sweetness.
Blend on high until the mixture is very smooth, about 1-2 minutes. Ensure there are no large chunks remaining.
Place a fine-mesh sieve or a nut milk bag over a glass or jar. Pour the blended mixture through the sieve/bag, pressing down on the solids with a spoon or spatula to extract all the liquid.
Discard the remaining pulp. Serve the ginger turmeric shot immediately, or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days.

recipes.title: Baked salmon
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper for easy cleanup.
Step 2: Pat the salmon fillets dry with a paper towel. This helps the skin crisp up and seasonings adhere better.
Step 3: Place the salmon fillets on the prepared baking sheet. Drizzle olive oil evenly over each fillet.
Step 4: Season the salmon with salt and black pepper. If desired, place lemon slices on top of each fillet and sprinkle with fresh dill.
Step 5: Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork. Cooking time may vary depending on the thickness of the fillets.
Step 6: Remove from oven and serve immediately with your favorite side dishes.

recipes.title: Fish in crazy water
ingredients: 4 (6 oz / 170g) white fish fillets (such as cod, snapper, or sea bass)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Pat the fish fillets dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper on both sides.

Step 2: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or wide pot over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Step 3: Add the halved cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften and burst.
Step 4: Pour in the white wine and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let it reduce by half, about 2-3 minutes.
Step 5: Add the fish stock or water to the skillet and bring the liquid to a gentle simmer. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.

Step 6: Carefully place the fish fillets into the simmering liquid. Cover the skillet and cook for 5-8 minutes, or until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork. Cooking time will vary depending on the thickness of the fish.
Step 7: Gently transfer the cooked fish to serving plates. Spoon the 'crazy water' sauce (tomatoes and broth) over the fish.
Step 8: Garnish with fresh chopped parsley and serve immediately, optionally with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Chinese tea eggs
steps: Boil eggs for 7-8 minutes, then cool in ice water. Gently crack the eggshells.
In a pot, melt rock sugar over medium heat until golden brown. Add the rest of the ingredients and enough water to cover the eggs. Stir well.
Add the cracked eggs and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, adding water if needed. Let the eggs sit in the broth for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Peel and serve!".

recipes.title: Yorkshire pudding
ingredients: 3-4 tablespoons beef dripping or vegetable oil
steps: Step 1: Sift the plain flour and salt into a large bowl.
Step 2: In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs until light and frothy.

Step 3: Gradually add the whisked eggs to the flour, mixing until a thick paste forms. Slowly whisk in the milk until you have a smooth, lump-free batter. Cover the bowl and let the batter rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or up to several hours.
Step 4: Preheat your oven to 220°C (425°F). Place 1 tablespoon of beef dripping or oil into each hole of a 4-hole Yorkshire pudding tin or 12-hole muffin tin.
Step 5: Place the tin with the fat into the preheated oven for 10-15 minutes, until the fat is smoking hot.
Step 6: Carefully remove the hot tin from the oven. Give the batter a final whisk and quickly pour it into the smoking hot fat, filling each hole about one-third full.

Step 7: Immediately return the tin to the oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the puddings are well-risen, golden brown, and crisp. Do not open the oven door during the first 15 minutes of cooking.
Step 8: Serve immediately as a side dish.

recipes.title: Berry yogurt bake
ingredients: 2 tbsp flour ( I used semolina flour but
steps: Preheat the oven to 180oc
In a bowl, whisk your eggs and add in the yoghurt, flour, honey and mixed berries.
Fold everything together well and transfer to a lined baking dish (I used a loaf pan but 8x8 would work well).
Top with extra berries and place into the oven for 35 - 40 minutes until golden brown and cooked through
Leave to cool slightly and serve with honey and extra berries

recipes.title: Chimichurri steak and potatoes
ingredients: 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil (for chimichurri)
steps: Step 1: Prepare the Chimichurri. Finely chop the parsley and cilantro. In a small bowl, combine the chopped herbs, minced garlic, red wine vinegar, 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil, red pepper flakes (if using), 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp black pepper. Stir well and set aside to allow flavors to meld.

Step 2: Prepare the Potatoes. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Halve the baby potatoes. On a large baking sheet, toss the potatoes with 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, paprika (if using), and garlic powder (if using). Spread them in a single layer.
Step 3: Roast the Potatoes. Roast the potatoes for 25-35 minutes, or until they are tender and golden brown, flipping them halfway through to ensure even cooking.
Step 4: Prepare the Steak. While the potatoes are roasting, pat the steak dry thoroughly with paper towels. Season generously on both sides with 1 tsp salt and 1/2 tsp black pepper.

Step 5: Cook the Steak. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet or heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat until shimmering. Carefully place the seasoned steak in the hot pan. Sear for 3-5 minutes per side for medium-rare, or to your desired doneness. Use a meat thermometer for accuracy (130-135°F for medium-rare).

Step 6: Rest the Steak. Remove the steak from the pan and transfer it to a cutting board. Let it rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing against the grain. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender steak.
Step 7: Serve. Once the potatoes are done and the steak has rested and been sliced, arrange them on plates. Drizzle generously with the prepared chimichurri sauce and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Salmon soup Nordic
ingredients: 500g salmon fillet, skinless and boneless, cut into 2-3 cm cubes
steps: Step 1: In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the diced carrots and sliced leek. Sauté for about 5 minutes until slightly softened.
Step 2: Pour in the fish stock. Add the diced potatoes and bay leaf (if using). Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the potatoes and carrots are tender.
Step 3: Add the salmon cubes to the soup. Pour in the heavy cream. Simmer gently for 3-5 minutes, or until the salmon is just cooked through. Do not boil vigorously after adding the salmon and cream.
Step 4: Remove the bay leaf. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in most of the fresh dill.
Step 5: Ladle the salmon soup into bowls and garnish with the remaining fresh dill before serving.

recipes.title: Pork adobo
ingredients: 2 lbs pork belly or shoulder, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
steps: Step 1: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat cooking oil over medium-high heat. Add pork cubes and brown on all sides. Remove pork from the pot and set aside.
Step 2: In the same pot, add the crushed garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Step 3: Return the browned pork to the pot. Add soy sauce, vinegar, bay leaves, and whole black peppercorns. If using, add water and sugar.
Step 4: Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot and simmer for 45-60 minutes, or until the pork is tender.
Step 5: Remove the lid, increase the heat to medium, and continue to simmer for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the sauce to reduce and thicken slightly. Stir occasionally.
Step 6: Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot with steamed rice.

recipes.title: One pot French onion soup
ingredients: 1/2 cup dry white wine (such as Sauvignon Blanc) or dry sherry
1 tsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: In a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot, melt butter and olive oil over medium-low heat. Add sliced onions and sugar. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 30-45 minutes, until onions are deeply caramelized and dark golden brown.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Sprinkle flour over the onions and stir, cooking for another 2 minutes.
Pour in the white wine (if using) and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Cook until the wine has almost evaporated.
Stir in the beef broth, thyme, and bay leaf. Bring the soup to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for at least 20-30 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Remove and discard the bay leaf. Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
To serve, preheat your broiler. Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls. Top each bowl with 1-2 slices of toasted baguette and a generous amount of shredded Gruyere cheese.
Place bowls on a baking sheet and broil for 2-4 minutes, or until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and golden brown. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Smoked salmon Dutch baby
ingredients: 1/2 lemon, cut into wedges, for serving
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Place a 10-inch cast iron skillet (or oven-safe pan) in the oven to heat for at least 10 minutes.
Step 2: In a medium bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, flour, and salt until smooth and no lumps remain.
Step 3: Carefully remove the hot skillet from the oven. Add the butter to the skillet and swirl until melted and coats the bottom and sides.
Step 4: Pour the batter into the hot, buttered skillet.
Step 5: Immediately return the skillet to the oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the Dutch baby is puffed, golden brown, and set in the center.
Step 6: While the Dutch baby bakes, prepare your toppings: arrange the smoked salmon, have the softened cream cheese ready, chop the fresh dill, and thinly slice the red onion.
Step 7: Once baked, remove the Dutch baby from the oven. Spread the softened cream cheese over the warm Dutch baby.
Step 8: Arrange the smoked salmon slices over the cream cheese. Sprinkle with chopped fresh dill, thinly sliced red onion, and capers (if using).
Step 9: Season with freshly ground black pepper and serve immediately with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Beancurd rolls
steps: To make the filling, blend tofu, mushrooms, and carrots in a food processor.
Lay a beancurd sheet on a surface, then add some filling to the middle, then wrap it like a tiny burrito.
Add oil to a pan, about half inch, then add the rolls and fry until golden brown, as you would a dumpling.
Use paper towls to absorb extra oil, then garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Swiss croissant bake
steps: Preheat oven to 350F (175C) and grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Layer the bottom of the dish with croissant halves. Sprinkle half the ham and Swiss cheese on top. Repeat with a second layer.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, Dijon mustard, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Pour evenly over the croissant layers.
Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake for an additional 1520 minutes, or until golden and set.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm. : Credit to @themoodyfoody Want to discover the Alpine "Water" hack that burns 57lbs of stubborn flab? Click the link in bio!

recipes.title: Galbi tang
ingredients: *2 green onions (white part for soup, green part for garnish)
steps: Optional - Soak the rib in cold water for 30 mins
Blanch short rib for 5 mins to get rid of impurities
Drain and rinse short rib with cold water until water runs clear
In a small pot, add short rib, onions, whites of green onions, radish, and garlic with cold water
Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 90 mins
Take out the onion, whites on green onion, garlic, and ginger out
Slice up radish into bite size pieces
Season broth to your liking
Cut short ribs into slices
Optional- add slices of egg

recipes.title: Stacked baked potatoes
ingredients: 3-4 medium-sized potatoes (Yukon Gold or Russets work best)
1 tsp fresh thyme leaves (or 12 tsp dried thyme)
14 cup grated Parmesan cheese (optional for extra flavor)
steps: Preheat oven to 375F (190C) and grease a muffin tin.
Slice potatoes thinly using a mandoline or sharp knife.
In a bowl, mix melted butter, garlic, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Toss potato slices in the butter mixture until well coated.
Stack the potato slices into the muffin tin, filling each cup almost to the top.
(Optional) Sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top for added flavor.
Bake for 45-50 minutes, or until edges are golden and crispy.

recipes.title: Nori wrapped tofu
steps: cloves garlic
/4 cup soy sauce
tbsp vegetarian oyster sauce
/2 cup water
tbsp cornstarch black pepper add scallions for garnish

recipes.title: Stuffed eggplant
ingredients: 1 lb ground beef (or lamb, or vegetarian substitute)
1 cup chopped eggplant flesh (from the scooped out eggplants)
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a baking dish.

Step 2: Cut eggplants in half lengthwise. Scoop out the flesh, leaving a 1/2-inch shell. Chop the scooped eggplant flesh into small pieces. Sprinkle the eggplant shells with salt and let them sit cut-side down on paper towels for 15 minutes to draw out excess moisture. Pat dry.
Step 3: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Add ground beef to the skillet and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat.

Step 5: Stir in the chopped eggplant flesh, diced tomatoes (with their juice), tomato sauce, parsley, oregano, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens slightly.
Step 6: Arrange the eggplant shells in the prepared baking dish. Spoon the meat mixture evenly into each eggplant shell.
Step 7: Sprinkle the stuffed eggplants with mozzarella and Parmesan cheeses.
Step 8: Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until the eggplant is tender and the cheese is melted and lightly golden brown. Serve hot.

recipes.title: Orange cake
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch round cake pan.
Step 2: In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
Step 3: In a large bowl, cream together the softened butter and granulated sugar with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
Step 4: Beat in the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Stir in the orange zest.
Step 5: In a separate small bowl, combine the orange juice and milk.

Step 6: Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, alternating with the orange juice mixture, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Step 7: Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and spread evenly.
Step 8: Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Step 9: Let the cake cool in the pan for 10 minutes before inverting it onto a wire rack to cool completely.

recipes.title: Shrimp oreganata
ingredients: 14 cup dry white wine (such as Pinot Grigio)
2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley, divided, plus more for garnish
1 lemon, cut into wedges for serving
steps: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminum foil and lightly spray with cooking spray.
Peel shrimp, leaving tails intact.
Use a paring knife to cut lengthwise down vein side of shrimp, cutting almost but not all the way through the shrimp. Remove the vein and gently press shrimp apart to butterfly it.

Combine shrimp, wine, olive oil, oregano, 1 tablespoon parsley, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, and 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper, and salt in a bowl; toss to coat shrimp evenly. Let stand at room temperature for at least 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, combine panko, Parmesan cheese, 2 tablespoons melted butter, and remaining 1 tablespoon parsley, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, and 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper in a small bowl; stir until well blended. : Karen Rankin : @nicolemcmom

recipes.title: Cracking carrot
ingredients: 1 lb carrots, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch sticks
1 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped (optional, for garnish)
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine the peeled and cut carrots with olive oil, salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika. Toss until the carrots are evenly coated.
Spread the seasoned carrots in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet.
Roast for 20-25 minutes, or until tender-crisp and slightly caramelized, flipping halfway through.
Remove from oven, transfer to a serving dish, and garnish with fresh parsley if desired. Serve warm.

recipes.title: Scallion oil crispy chicken
ingredients: 2 lbs boneless, skin-on chicken thighs (about 8 pieces)
steps: Pat chicken thighs dry. In a large bowl, combine chicken with grated ginger, minced garlic, soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, sesame oil, white pepper, sugar, and 1/2 tsp salt. Mix well and marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 4 hours in the refrigerator.

While chicken marinates, prepare scallion oil. Slice 8-10 scallion stalks into 2-inch pieces. Heat 1.5 cups vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Add scallion pieces and cook slowly, stirring occasionally, until they are deeply golden brown and fragrant, about 15-20 minutes. Strain the oil into a heatproof bowl, discarding the crispy scallions (or save for garnish).
In a shallow dish, whisk together all-purpose flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and remaining 1/2 tsp salt.
Heat the remaining 1.5 cups vegetable oil (or use the prepared scallion oil if preferred for frying) in a large Dutch oven or deep skillet to 350-375°F (175-190°C).
Dredge each marinated chicken thigh in the flour mixture, pressing to coat thoroughly, then shake off any excess.

Carefully place chicken thighs into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding. Fry for 6-8 minutes per side, or until golden brown, crispy, and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F/74°C).
Transfer fried chicken to a wire rack set over a paper towel-lined baking sheet to drain excess oil.
Drizzle the crispy chicken generously with the prepared scallion oil. Garnish with thinly sliced fresh scallions (from the remaining stalks) before serving.

recipes.title: Crme caramel
steps: g sugar
Pour the milk and the vanilla bean (previously split in half) into a saucepan.
Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Let it cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine the eggs and sugar, mixing with a hand whisk until combined. No need to incorporate air.
Remove the vanilla bean from the now lukewarm milk, and add the fresh cream.
Add the milk to the egg and sugar mixture, stirring again until you get a smooth cream.
Prepare the caramel by heating the sugar in a small pan without stirring, just rotating the pan.
When the caramel turns amber, pour it into 6 molds placed in a baking dish, covering their base.
Add the cream mixture to fill the molds.
Pour boiling water into the baking dish until it covers one-third of the molds.
Bake at 180C for about 50 minutes. Will you try it?

recipes.title: Golden milk
ingredients: 1 cup milk (dairy or non-dairy like almond or coconut)
1/4 tsp ground ginger (or a small slice of fresh ginger)
1 tsp maple syrup or honey (or to taste)
steps: Step 1: In a small saucepan, combine the milk, turmeric, ginger, cinnamon (if using), black pepper, and coconut oil (if using).
Step 2: Heat the mixture over medium-low heat, whisking gently, until it's hot and steamy but not boiling. This usually takes about 5-7 minutes.
Step 3: Remove from heat and stir in the maple syrup or honey.
Step 4: Pour the golden milk into a mug. For a smoother drink, you can strain it or use an immersion blender to froth it slightly. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Fish poached in tomato and dill
ingredients: 1 leek, white part only, sliced thin
1 bunch of dill, tops roughly chopped
