1 cup broth (any type of broth works here, I used a chicken broth)
1 small cube of butter, less than 1 Tbsp
1 Tbsp lemon juice (I used Meyer lemon)
steps: Saute leeks, garlic and tomatoes in avocado oil
Add lemon juice, fish sauce, butter
Poach halibut in it for 3 min at a gentle simmer and then turn off heat and let it sit for 10 minutes until the filet is cooked through

Serve with rice and greens of your choice, or crusty bread! xo Dr. Michelle Citations: Wang L et al. The effects of advanced glycation end-products on skin and potential anti-glycation strategies. Exp Dermatol. 2024 PMID: 38563644. Gutierrez-Mariscal FM et al. Modulation of circulating levels of advanced glycation end products and its impact on intima-media thickness of both common carotid arteries: CORDIOPREV randomised controlled trial. Cardiovasc Diabetol. 2024 PMID: 39402581; PMCID: PMC11475769. .

recipes.title: One pot clam and cod
ingredients: 2 cups clam juice or fish broth
1.5 lbs cod fillets, cut into 2-inch pieces
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

Step 2: Pour in white wine and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Add clam juice (or fish broth), diced tomatoes, and quartered potatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until potatoes are almost tender.

Step 3: Gently add the cod pieces and scrubbed clams to the pot. Cover and continue to simmer for 5-8 minutes, or until the clams have opened and the cod is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork. Discard any clams that do not open.
Step 4: Stir in the fresh spinach (or kale) and chopped parsley. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the spinach wilts.
Step 5: Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Braised chicken
ingredients: 2 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs or drumsticks
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Season chicken generously with salt and pepper.
Step 2: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium-high heat. Brown chicken on all sides until golden, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove chicken from pot and set aside.
Step 3: Add chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Pour in white wine (if using) and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Cook until the wine has mostly evaporated, about 2-3 minutes.
Step 5: Stir in chicken broth, diced tomatoes (with their juice), dried thyme, and bay leaf. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Step 6: Return the browned chicken to the pot, nestling it into the liquid and vegetables. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged.

Step 7: Cover the pot and either reduce heat to low and simmer on the stovetop for 45-60 minutes, or transfer to a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) and braise for 45-60 minutes, or until chicken is very tender and cooked through.
Step 8: Remove bay leaf. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed. Garnish with fresh parsley, if desired, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Broccoli cheddar soup
steps: /2 tbsp smoked paprika
c chopped raw brocolli
c milk
/2 c shredded white cheddar
In a dutch oven on medium heat add your butter. Once melted add in your onions and sautee for 2-3 minutes
Add in garlic and sautee for another minute before adding in your carrots and flour.
Stir until combined and add in your broth and seasonings and sautee for another 2 minutes
Add in your brocolli and milk and stir before adding in your cheese. Cook on low for 5-7 kinutes or until the brocolli is tender

recipes.title: Miso braised short ribs
steps: 12  Tbsp Graza Sizzle Olive Oil 13  c packed brown sugar
12  Tbsp maple syrup
head garlic, 14 inch top removed
12-in ginger, cut into coins
Tbsp garlic, minced
bunch green onion, green + white parts only 12 c sake

Tbsp shallot, finely chopped
Tbsp fish sauce
Tbsp lemon juice
Tbsp Graza Drizzle Olive Oil
12 tsp garlic, minced
tsp ginger, finely chopped
12 tsp sugar

recipes.title: Kue dads
steps: Step 1: Grease a small steaming mold or heat-proof dish (approximately 6-inch round or square).
Step 2: In a large bowl, whisk together the rice flour, all-purpose flour, granulated sugar, and salt.
Step 3: In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, then gradually add the thick coconut milk and pandan paste (if using), mixing until well combined.
Step 4: Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking continuously until a smooth batter forms with no lumps.
Step 5: Pour the batter into the greased mold.
Step 6: Prepare a steamer and bring water to a rolling boil over medium-high heat.
Step 7: Carefully place the mold with the batter into the steamer. Cover the lid with a cloth to prevent condensation from dripping onto the cake.
Step 8: Steam for 25-30 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Step 9: Carefully remove the mold from the steamer and let it cool slightly before unmolding and slicing to serve.

recipes.title: Spaghetti ala boloesa
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Freshly grated Parmesan cheese, for serving
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add ground beef to the pot, breaking it up with a spoon. Cook until browned, draining any excess fat.
Stir in the finely diced carrot and celery. Cook for 5-7 minutes until slightly softened.
Pour in the red wine (if using) and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until reduced.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, beef broth, dried oregano, and dried basil. Season with salt and pepper.
Bring the sauce to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and let it cook for at least 45 minutes, stirring occasionally. For a richer flavor, simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours.
About 15 minutes before the sauce is ready, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente.
Drain the spaghetti and serve immediately with the Bolognese sauce. Garnish with freshly grated Parmesan cheese.

recipes.title: Galbi Jim short ribs
ingredients: 1 Asian pear, cut into large chunks
steps: Place the beef short ribs in a pot and add enough cold water to cover them by 1 inch. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Discard the murky water, rinse the beef thoroughly with warm water, and remove any scum. Wash the pot and return the beef ribs to it.
In a blender, combine the onion, garlic, ginger, Asian pear, soy sauce, Korean plum syrup, beef broth, black pepper, and sesame oil. Blend until smooth.
Pour the sauce over the beef ribs. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce to low. Cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Add the chestnuts, shiitake mushrooms, carrots, and Korean radish. Bring to a boil again, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for another hour, stirring occasionally.

recipes.title: Creamy broccoli soup
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in broccoli florets and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until broccoli is very tender.
Carefully transfer the soup to a blender (or use an immersion blender directly in the pot) and blend until completely smooth and creamy.
Return the soup to the pot (if using a regular blender). Stir in milk or cream.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Heat gently until warmed through, but do not boil after adding milk/cream.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with shredded cheese or a drizzle of cream.

recipes.title: White bean soup
ingredients: 3 (15-ounce) cans cannellini beans, rinsed and drained
1/2 cup small pasta (like ditalini or orzo), optional
2 cups fresh spinach or kale, chopped
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 8-10 minutes.
Step 2: Add minced garlic, dried thyme, dried rosemary, and bay leaf. Cook for another minute until fragrant.
Step 3: Pour in the vegetable broth and add the rinsed cannellini beans. Bring the soup to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

Step 4: Remove the bay leaf. Using an immersion blender, or by carefully transferring 2-3 cups of the soup to a regular blender, blend a portion of the soup until creamy. Return the blended portion to the pot (if using a regular blender) to thicken the soup.
Step 5: If using pasta, add it to the soup and cook according to package directions until al dente, usually 7-10 minutes.
Step 6: Stir in the fresh spinach or kale and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Step 7: Season the soup with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley, if desired, before serving.

recipes.title: Fluffy blueberry yogurt pancakes
steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
Step 2: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the egg, yogurt, milk, and vanilla extract until well combined.
Step 3: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Stir gently with a spatula until just combined. Do not overmix; a few lumps are okay.
Step 4: Gently fold in the blueberries.
Step 5: Heat a lightly greased griddle or large non-stick pan over medium heat.
Step 6: Pour ¼ cup of batter per pancake onto the hot griddle. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Step 7: Serve immediately with your favorite toppings.

recipes.title: Balsamic braised short ribs
ingredients: 1.5 cups dry red wine (e.g., Cabernet Sauvignon)
0.5 tsp black pepper, or to taste
steps: Step 1: Pat short ribs dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper.
Step 2: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium-high heat. Sear ribs in batches until well browned on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove ribs and set aside.
Step 3: Add chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Step 4: Add minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Step 5: Pour in red wine and balsamic vinegar. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, allowing the alcohol to cook off slightly.
Step 6: Return the short ribs to the pot. Add beef broth, bay leaves, and thyme sprigs. Ensure ribs are mostly submerged; add more broth if needed.
Step 7: Bring the liquid to a gentle simmer, then cover the pot and transfer to a preheated oven at 325°F (160°C).
Step 8: Braise for 2.5 to 3 hours, or until the meat is very tender and easily pulls away from the bone.
Step 9: Carefully remove ribs from the pot and set aside. Skim any excess fat from the surface of the braising liquid. Remove bay leaves and thyme sprigs.
Step 10: (Optional) For a thicker sauce, bring the braising liquid to a simmer on the stovetop and reduce until desired consistency is reached. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Step 11: Serve the short ribs with the sauce, over mashed potatoes, polenta, or pasta.

recipes.title: Apricot and balsamic roasted chicken
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh rosemary, chopped (or 1 tsp dried)
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Step 2: In a medium bowl, whisk together the apricot preserves, balsamic vinegar, olive oil, minced garlic, chopped rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Step 3: Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels. Place them in a large roasting pan or oven-safe dish. Add the red onion wedges around the chicken, if using.
Step 4: Pour the apricot-balsamic glaze over the chicken and onions, ensuring the chicken is well coated.

Step 5: Roast for 35-45 minutes, or until the internal temperature of the chicken reaches 165°F (74°C) and the skin is golden and slightly caramelized. Baste the chicken with the pan juices once or twice during cooking.
Step 6: Remove from oven, tent loosely with foil, and let rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Lemon grass pork chop
ingredients: 4 boneless pork chops (about 1-inch thick, 1.5 lbs total)
2 stalks fresh lemongrass, tough outer layers removed, tender inner part finely minced (about 2 tbsp)
steps: Step 1: In a medium bowl, combine the minced lemongrass, minced garlic, minced shallot, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and black pepper. Mix well to create the marinade.

Step 2: Place the pork chops in a shallow dish or a resealable plastic bag. Pour the marinade over the pork chops, ensuring they are evenly coated. Marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.
Step 3: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.

Step 4: Remove the pork chops from the marinade (discarding any excess marinade) and place them in the hot pan. Cook for 4-6 minutes per side, depending on thickness, until golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 145°F / 63°C).
Step 5: Transfer the cooked pork chops to a cutting board and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving. Serve hot with rice and a side of vegetables.

recipes.title: Stuffed cabbage
ingredients: 1.5 lbs ground beef (or a mix of beef and pork)
1 teaspoon salt, plus more to taste
1/2 teaspoon black pepper, plus more to taste
steps: Prepare the cabbage: Core the cabbage. In a large pot of boiling salted water, carefully place the whole cabbage. Boil for 10-15 minutes, or until the outer leaves soften and can be easily peeled off. Remove softened leaves and continue boiling the remaining cabbage until all large leaves are pliable. Trim the thickest part of the central vein from each leaf.

Prepare the filling: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, uncooked rice, half of the chopped onion, minced garlic, 1/4 cup of the tomato sauce, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon black pepper, and paprika. Mix thoroughly with your hands until well combined.

Assemble the rolls: Lay a cabbage leaf flat. Place about 1/4 cup of the meat mixture near the stem end. Fold in the sides of the leaf over the filling, then roll tightly from the stem end towards the tip. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.

Prepare the sauce: In a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the remaining half of the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, remaining tomato sauce, beef broth, and dried dill (if using). Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.

Arrange and cook: Carefully place the stuffed cabbage rolls seam-side down into the simmering sauce, packing them snugly. Ensure the rolls are mostly submerged in the sauce. If needed, add a little more broth or water.
Simmer: Bring the sauce back to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 2 to 2.5 hours, or until the cabbage is very tender and the rice is fully cooked.
Serve: Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot, optionally with a dollop of sour cream.

recipes.title: Baby lemon pie
ingredients: ½ cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, cold and cubed
½ cup fresh lemon juice (from 2-3 lemons)
steps: Step 1: Make the Crust: In a food processor, pulse flour, ¼ cup sugar, and salt. Add cold butter and pulse until coarse crumbs form. Add egg yolk and 2 tablespoons ice water, pulse until dough just comes together. If too dry, add more ice water 1 tsp at a time. Form into a disc, wrap in plastic, and chill for at least 30 minutes.

Step 2: Prepare Pie Shells: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to about ⅛-inch thickness. Cut out circles using a cookie cutter slightly larger than your mini pie tins (e.g., 3-inch tins). Gently press dough into 6-8 mini pie tins. Prick the bottoms with a fork.

Step 3: Blind Bake Crusts: Line each crust with parchment paper or foil and fill with pie weights or dried beans. Bake for 10-12 minutes, then remove weights and parchment/foil and bake for another 5-7 minutes, or until lightly golden. Remove from oven and reduce oven temperature to 325°F (160°C).
Step 4: Make the Lemon Filling: While crusts are baking, whisk together lemon juice, lemon zest, ½ cup sugar, and eggs in a medium bowl until well combined. Gradually whisk in the melted butter.
Step 5: Bake the Pies: Pour the lemon filling into the warm, blind-baked crusts. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the filling is set but still slightly wobbly in the center.
Step 6: Cool and Chill: Let the pies cool completely on a wire rack. Once cooled, transfer to the refrigerator and chill for at least 1 hour before serving.

recipes.title: Oxtail pho
ingredients: 1.5 oz rock sugar (or 3 tbsp granulated sugar)
2 limes, cut into wedges, for serving
2-3 jalapeños or serrano peppers, thinly sliced, for serving
1 small white onion, thinly sliced, for serving
steps: Step 1: Place oxtail in a large pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a rolling boil. Boil for 10-15 minutes to remove impurities. Drain, rinse oxtail thoroughly under cold water, and clean the pot.
Step 2: While oxtail boils, char the halved onion and ginger directly over a gas flame, under a broiler, or in a dry pan until softened and slightly blackened, about 10-15 minutes.

Step 3: In a dry skillet over medium heat, toast star anise, cinnamon sticks, black cardamom, cloves, coriander seeds, and fennel seeds until fragrant, about 2-3 minutes. Place toasted spices in a reusable spice bag or cheesecloth.

Step 4: Return cleaned oxtail to the large pot. Add charred onion and ginger, the spice bag, fish sauce, rock sugar, and salt. Pour in 12-14 cups of fresh water. Bring to a boil, then immediately reduce heat to low, cover partially, and simmer for 3-5 hours, or until the oxtail is very tender and falling off the bone. Skim any foam or excess fat from the surface periodically.

Step 5: Carefully remove the oxtail from the pot. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the charred aromatics and spice bag. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Skim any remaining fat from the broth.
Step 6: Once the oxtail is cool enough to handle, remove the meat from the bones and shred or cut into bite-sized pieces. Discard bones.

Step 7: Cook pho rice noodles according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and rinse briefly with cold water to prevent sticking. Arrange bean sprouts, fresh basil, cilantro, mint, lime wedges, sliced jalapeños, thinly sliced white onion, chopped green onions, hoisin sauce, and sriracha on a serving platter.
Step 8: To serve, place a portion of cooked rice noodles in each serving bowl. Top with shredded oxtail meat. Ladle hot, simmering broth over the noodles and meat.
Step 9: Serve immediately with the prepared garnishes on the side, allowing each person to customize their bowl.

recipes.title: Eggplant dish
steps: Step 1: Slice the eggplant into 1/2-inch thick rounds. Sprinkle with 1/2 tsp salt and let sit for 15-20 minutes to draw out moisture, then pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.

Step 2: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry eggplant slices in batches until golden brown on both sides and tender, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside on a plate lined with paper towels.
Step 3: In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Add minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Pour in the crushed tomatoes, add the remaining 1/2 tsp salt and 1/4 tsp black pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Step 5: Stir in the fresh basil.
Step 6: Arrange the fried eggplant slices on a serving platter and spoon the tomato sauce generously over them.
Step 7: Garnish with grated Parmesan cheese if desired and serve warm.

recipes.title: Pulpo patata
ingredients: 1.5 kg whole octopus (fresh or frozen, thawed)
1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil, plus more for serving
1 tbsp sweet paprika (pimentón dulce de la Vera)
1/2 tsp hot paprika (pimentón picante de la Vera, optional)
steps: Step 1: If using frozen octopus, thaw it completely. If fresh, clean it thoroughly under cold running water, removing any internal organs and eyes.

Step 2: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Hold the octopus by the head and dip the tentacles into the boiling water for 10 seconds, then remove. Repeat this 'scaring' process two more times. This helps tenderize the octopus.

Step 3: Submerge the octopus completely in the boiling water. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for 30-50 minutes, or until very tender when pierced with a skewer (cooking time depends on the size of the octopus).
Step 4: While the octopus cooks, wash the potatoes. You can cook them in the same pot as the octopus for added flavor, or in a separate pot of salted water. Cook until tender, about 20-30 minutes.

Step 5: Once the octopus is cooked, remove it from the water and let it rest on a cutting board for 10 minutes. Remove the potatoes from the water, peel them (if desired), and slice them into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
Step 6: Using kitchen shears, cut the octopus tentacles into 1/2-inch thick slices. Discard the head or slice it if desired.
Step 7: Arrange the sliced potatoes on a large serving platter or individual plates. Top with the sliced octopus.
Step 8: Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil. Sprinkle with coarse sea salt, sweet paprika, and hot paprika (if using). Serve warm.

recipes.title: Pizza smash potatoes
ingredients: 1.5 lbs small potatoes (e.g., baby Yukon Gold or red potatoes)
1/4 cup mini pepperoni or sliced regular pepperoni
Fresh basil or oregano for garnish (optional)
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Step 2: Place potatoes in a large pot and cover with cold water. Add a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, about 15-20 minutes. Drain well.

Step 3: Arrange the cooked potatoes on the prepared baking sheet, leaving some space between them. Using the bottom of a glass or a potato masher, gently smash each potato until flattened to about 1/2 inch thick.
Step 4: Drizzle the smashed potatoes with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, black pepper, and garlic powder (if using).
Step 5: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy.
Step 6: Remove the baking sheet from the oven. Spoon about 1 teaspoon of marinara sauce onto each smashed potato, then sprinkle with shredded mozzarella cheese and top with pepperoni.
Step 7: Return to the oven and bake for another 5-8 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the pepperoni is slightly crispy.
Step 8: Garnish with fresh basil or oregano, if desired, and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Chicken with 40 garlic cloves
ingredients: 1/2 cup dry white wine or chicken broth
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Step 2: Pat the chicken dry with paper towels. Season generously inside and out with salt and pepper.
Step 3: In a large roasting pan or Dutch oven, toss the chicken with the unpeeled garlic cloves, olive oil, rosemary, and thyme. Ensure the chicken is coated and the garlic is distributed around it.
Step 4: Pour the white wine or chicken broth into the bottom of the pan.

Step 5: Roast for 60-90 minutes, or until the chicken is golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F / 74°C in the thickest part of the thigh). Baste occasionally with pan juices.
Step 6: If the chicken is browning too quickly, loosely tent it with foil.
Step 7: Remove from oven, transfer chicken to a cutting board, and let rest for 10 minutes before carving. The garlic cloves will be soft and spreadable.
Step 8: Serve the carved chicken with the roasted garlic cloves and pan juices.

recipes.title: Pandan cake
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch round cake pan or line with parchment paper.
Step 2: In a large bowl, sift together the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
Step 3: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the eggs and granulated sugar until light and fluffy, about 3-5 minutes.
Step 4: Gradually add the coconut milk, pandan extract, and vegetable oil to the egg mixture, mixing until just combined.
Step 5: Slowly add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing with a spatula or whisk until just combined and no lumps remain. Be careful not to overmix.
Step 6: Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and spread evenly.
Step 7: Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Step 8: Let the cake cool in the pan for 10 minutes before inverting it onto a wire rack to cool completely.

recipes.title: Creamy polenta
ingredients: 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese (or other hard cheese)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, bring the water or broth and salt to a rolling boil over medium-high heat.
Slowly whisk in the polenta in a steady stream to prevent lumps. Continue whisking for 1-2 minutes until fully incorporated.

Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer, stirring frequently with a wooden spoon to prevent sticking, for 20-30 minutes, or until the polenta is thick and creamy. If it becomes too thick, add a splash more hot water or broth.
Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the butter and Parmesan cheese until melted and fully combined.
Season with freshly ground black pepper to taste. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Morocon Aubergine Dip
ingredients: 3 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for drizzling
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Pierce the aubergines several times with a fork. Drizzle with a little olive oil and place on a baking tray.
Step 2: Roast the aubergines for 35-45 minutes, or until very soft and the skin is shrivelled and slightly charred. Alternatively, you can grill them over an open flame until charred and soft.
Step 3: Once cooked, remove from the oven and let cool slightly. Cut the aubergines in half lengthwise and scoop out the flesh into a bowl, discarding the skin.
Step 4: Mash the aubergine flesh with a fork until it reaches your desired consistency (chunky or smooth).
Step 5: In a separate small pan, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and chopped red onion and sauté for 3-5 minutes until softened and fragrant.

Step 6: Add the sautéed garlic and onion to the mashed aubergine. Stir in the fresh parsley, fresh coriander, lemon juice, ground cumin, smoked paprika, ground coriander, and cayenne pepper (if using).
Step 7: Drizzle with the remaining 2 tbsp olive oil. Season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Mix well.
Step 8: Serve immediately or chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes for the flavours to meld. Serve with pita bread, crackers, or fresh vegetables.

recipes.title: Eggplant Shakshuka
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1/4 cup fresh parsley or cilantro, chopped, for garnish
steps: Step 1: Dice the eggplant into 1/2-inch cubes. Place in a colander, sprinkle with 1/2 teaspoon salt, and let sit for 15-20 minutes to draw out moisture. Pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.

Step 2: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large, deep skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add the dried eggplant and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8-10 minutes. Remove the eggplant from the skillet and set aside.

Step 3: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the skillet. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and diced red bell pepper (if using) and cook for another 3-5 minutes until fragrant and tender.
Step 4: Stir in the tomato paste, cumin, smoked paprika, chili powder (if using), sugar (if using), 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Step 5: Pour in the crushed tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Step 6: Stir the cooked eggplant back into the tomato sauce.
Step 7: Using the back of a spoon, make 4-6 wells in the sauce. Carefully crack one egg into each well.
Step 8: Cover the skillet and cook for 5-8 minutes, or until the egg whites are set and the yolks are cooked to your desired doneness (runny yolks are traditional).
Step 9: Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley or cilantro and serve immediately, often with crusty bread for dipping.

recipes.title: Baked Dijon Chicken
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Lightly grease a baking dish.
Step 2: Pat chicken breasts dry with paper towels. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Step 3: In a shallow dish, spread the Dijon mustard.
Step 4: In another shallow dish, combine breadcrumbs, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Step 5: Dip each chicken breast into the Dijon mustard, ensuring it's fully coated.
Step 6: Then, dredge each mustard-coated chicken breast into the breadcrumb mixture, pressing gently to adhere the crumbs.
Step 7: Place the coated chicken breasts in the prepared baking dish. Drizzle lightly with olive oil.
Step 8: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Step 9: Let rest for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired.

recipes.title: Rustic rosemary bread
ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive oil, plus more for greasing
steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy.
Step 2: Stir in the 2 tablespoons of olive oil and chopped fresh rosemary.
Step 3: In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour and salt.
Step 4: Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing with a wooden spoon or spatula until a shaggy dough forms.
Step 5: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Step 6: Lightly oil a clean bowl. Place the dough in the bowl, turning once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
Step 7: Gently punch down the dough. Shape it into a round or oval loaf.
Step 8: Place the shaped dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rise for another 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.
Step 9: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) during the second rise.
Step 10: Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 200-210°F (93-99°C).
Step 11: Let the bread cool completely on a wire rack before slicing and serving.

recipes.title: Kue dadar
ingredients: 1 tsp pandan paste (or 2-3 drops green food coloring + pandan essence)
steps: Step 1: Prepare the filling (Unti Kelapa). In a saucepan, combine grated coconut, chopped palm sugar, water, knotted pandan leaf, and 1/4 tsp salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the liquid is absorbed and the mixture is slightly sticky and fragrant, about 10-15 minutes. Remove the pandan leaf and let the filling cool completely.

Step 2: Prepare the pancake batter. In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour and 1/2 tsp salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, coconut milk, and pandan paste (or food coloring/essence). Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking continuously until a smooth, lump-free batter is formed.

Step 3: Cook the pancakes. Heat a non-stick pan (about 18-20 cm diameter) over medium-low heat. Lightly grease if necessary. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the pan, tilting and swirling to coat the bottom evenly with a thin layer. Cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the edges curl slightly and the surface is set and no longer wet. Do not flip. Remove the cooked pancake and place it on a plate. Repeat with the remaining batter.

Step 4: Assemble the Kue Dadar. Lay a cooked pancake flat with the slightly rougher side facing up. Place about 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled coconut filling near one edge. Fold in the left and right sides of the pancake over the filling, then roll it up tightly from the bottom to form a cylinder.
Step 5: Serve. Arrange the assembled Kue Dadar on a serving plate. Serve immediately or store in an airtight container.

recipes.title: Orange ricotta poundcake
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9x5 inch loaf pan or a bundt pan.
Step 2: In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Step 3: In a large bowl, using an electric mixer, cream the softened butter until light and fluffy. Gradually add the granulated sugar and beat until well combined.
Step 4: Beat in the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
Step 5: Stir in the ricotta cheese, orange zest, and vanilla extract until just combined.

Step 6: Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, alternating with the orange juice, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Step 7: Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
Step 8: Bake for 60-70 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean. If the top browns too quickly, tent loosely with foil.
Step 9: Let the poundcake cool in the pan for 15 minutes before inverting onto a wire rack to cool completely.
Step 10: While the cake cools, prepare the glaze: In a small bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar and 2-3 tablespoons of fresh orange juice until smooth.
Step 11: Once the cake is completely cool, drizzle the glaze over the top.

recipes.title: Lemon poset
steps: Step 1: In a medium saucepan, combine the double cream and caster sugar.
Step 2: Heat gently over a medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has completely dissolved.
Step 3: Once the sugar is dissolved, bring the mixture to a rolling boil and let it boil for exactly 3 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent it from catching on the bottom.
Step 4: Remove the saucepan from the heat. Stir in the lemon juice and lemon zest.
Step 5: Pour the mixture into individual serving glasses, ramekins, or small bowls.
Step 6: Allow to cool slightly at room temperature, then transfer to the refrigerator and chill for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, until set.

recipes.title: Salmon clay pot rice
ingredients: 1 (6 oz) salmon fillet, skin on or off
1/2 inch fresh ginger, grated or thinly sliced
recipes.notes: Ingredients not detected automatically.
recipes.descriptionPreview: A single serving sizzling pot rice, seasoned with dried shrimp, shiitake mushrooms, and topped with soy marinated salmon and a scallion sauce vinaigrette.
steps: 1. Wash rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well and set aside.
2. Pat the salmon fillet dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, sesame oil, and grated ginger. Marinate the salmon in this mixture for at least 10 minutes.
3. In a clay pot, combine the washed rice, water (or broth), and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
4. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly with its lid, and cook for 10 minutes.

5. Carefully remove the lid and gently place the marinated salmon fillet on top of the partially cooked rice. Re-cover the pot and continue to cook for another 10-15 minutes, or until the rice has absorbed all the liquid and the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily.
6. Turn off the heat and let the clay pot sit, covered, for 5-10 minutes to allow the rice to steam and develop a crispy bottom (if desired).
7. Garnish with chopped scallions before serving directly from the clay pot.

recipes.title: Polenta and beer mushrooms
ingredients: 1 cup beer (e.g., amber ale or stout)
1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese (optional, for polenta)
steps: Clean and slice the mushrooms.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 8-10 minutes.
Pour in the beer and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let it reduce by half, about 5-7 minutes.
Add vegetable broth and fresh thyme. Season with salt and pepper. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
While the mushrooms are simmering, bring 4 cups of water or broth to a boil in a separate saucepan. Gradually whisk in the instant polenta, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
Reduce heat to low and continue to stir for 3-5 minutes, or until the polenta is creamy and cooked through. Stir in butter and grated Parmesan cheese (if using). Season with salt to taste.
Ladle the creamy polenta into bowls and top generously with the beer mushrooms. Garnish with fresh parsley.

recipes.title: Asparagus orzo
ingredients: 1 lb asparagus, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese, plus more for serving
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Bring a medium pot of salted water to a boil. Add orzo and cook according to package directions until al dente, about 8-10 minutes. Drain well and set aside.
While the orzo cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet or pan over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn.
Add the trimmed and cut asparagus to the skillet. Sauté for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the asparagus is tender-crisp.
Add the cooked and drained orzo to the skillet with the asparagus and garlic.
Pour in the vegetable broth, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, allowing the orzo to absorb some of the liquid and flavors.
Remove from heat and stir in the 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese.
Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan cheese if desired.

recipes.title: Kue dadar
ingredients: 1 tsp pandan paste (or green food coloring + pandan essence)
1 tbsp vegetable oil (for greasing pan, optional)
steps: Step 1: Prepare the filling. In a saucepan, combine grated coconut, chopped palm sugar, water, knotted pandan leaf (if using), and 1/4 tsp salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar melts, the mixture thickens, and most of the liquid has evaporated (about 10-15 minutes). Remove the pandan leaf and let the filling cool completely.

Step 2: Prepare the skin batter. In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour and 1/2 tsp salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, coconut milk, and pandan paste (or food coloring/essence). Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking continuously until a smooth, lump-free batter is formed.

Step 3: Cook the pancakes. Heat a non-stick frying pan or crepe pan over medium-low heat. Lightly grease the pan with a little oil if needed. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the pan, tilting and swirling quickly to coat the bottom evenly with a thin layer. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the edges start to curl and the surface is set and no longer wet. No need to flip. Remove the cooked pancake and place it on a plate. Repeat with the remaining batter.

Step 4: Assemble the Kue Dadar. Place one cooked pancake on a clean surface. Put about 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled coconut filling near one edge. Fold in the sides of the pancake, then roll it up tightly from the edge with the filling.
Step 5: Arrange the finished Kue Dadar on a serving plate and serve immediately or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Two minute cookie dough pudding
steps: In a microwave-safe mug or small bowl, melt the butter in the microwave for 15-20 seconds.
Add granulated sugar, brown sugar, vanilla extract, and milk to the melted butter. Stir until well combined.
Add the flour and salt to the mixture. Stir until just combined and no dry streaks remain. Do not overmix.
Fold in the mini chocolate chips.
Microwave on high for 45-60 seconds, or until the pudding is set but still gooey in the center. Cooking time may vary depending on microwave wattage.
Let cool for a minute before serving.

recipes.title: Mini burnt basque cheesecake
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line 6-8 mini springform pans (4-inch) or ramekins with parchment paper, allowing it to extend above the rim.
Step 2: In a large bowl, beat the softened cream cheese and granulated sugar with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, about 2-3 minutes.
Step 3: Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition until just combined. Do not overmix.
Step 4: Gradually pour in the heavy cream, vanilla extract (if using), and salt, mixing on low speed until just incorporated.
Step 5: Sift in the all-purpose flour and mix until no lumps remain, being careful not to incorporate too much air.
Step 6: Divide the batter evenly among the prepared mini pans.
Step 7: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the tops are deeply caramelized and almost black, and the centers are still jiggly when gently shaken.
Step 8: Remove from oven and let cool completely at room temperature. The cheesecakes will deflate as they cool.
Step 9: Once cooled, refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.

recipes.title: Apricot balsamic glazed chicken
ingredients: 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken breasts or thighs
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat chicken dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper.

Step 2: In a small saucepan, combine apricot preserves, balsamic vinegar, soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, and red pepper flakes (if using). Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the glaze thickens slightly, about 5-7 minutes. Remove from heat.
Step 3: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and sear for 3-4 minutes per side, until golden brown.
Step 4: Pour about half of the apricot balsamic glaze over the chicken in the skillet. Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven.
Step 5: Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the internal temperature of the chicken reaches 165°F (74°C), basting with the remaining glaze every 5-7 minutes during baking.
Step 6: Remove chicken from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Braised pork belly
ingredients: 1.5 kg pork belly (skin on, boneless), cut into 1.5-2 inch cubes
4-5 cups water or chicken stock (enough to cover)
steps: Step 1: Blanch the pork belly. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the pork belly cubes and blanch for 5-7 minutes to remove impurities. Drain well and rinse with cold water. Pat dry.

Step 2: (Optional) Sear the pork. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1 tbsp cooking oil over medium-high heat. Sear the pork belly cubes on all sides until lightly browned. Remove any excess rendered fat.
Step 3: Add aromatics. Add the ginger slices and scallion pieces to the pot and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Step 4: Add seasonings. Pour in the Shaoxing wine, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, rock sugar, star anise, and cinnamon stick. Stir to combine.

Step 5: Braise. Add enough water or chicken stock to just cover the pork belly. Bring the liquid to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the pork belly is fork-tender and the sauce has thickened slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Step 6: Reduce sauce (optional). If the sauce is too thin, remove the lid during the last 15-20 minutes of cooking and increase the heat slightly to reduce it to your desired consistency.
Step 7: Serve. Remove the star anise and cinnamon stick before serving. Serve hot with steamed rice and blanched greens.

recipes.title: Turkish eggs
ingredients: 1 tsp Aleppo pepper (or 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes)
1 tbsp fresh dill or mint, chopped (for garnish)
A splash of white vinegar (optional, for poaching)
steps: In a small bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt. Mix well and spread evenly on the bottom of a serving plate or shallow bowl.
Fill a medium saucepan with water and bring to a gentle simmer. Add a pinch of salt and a splash of white vinegar (if using). Carefully crack each egg into a separate small bowl or ramekin.
Gently slide the eggs into the simmering water. Cook for 3-4 minutes for runny yolks, or longer for a firmer yolk. Remove the poached eggs with a slotted spoon and drain any excess water.
While the eggs are poaching, melt the butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once melted and slightly foamy, remove from heat and stir in the Aleppo pepper (or red pepper flakes).
Carefully place the poached eggs on top of the garlic yogurt. Drizzle generously with the warm chili butter.
Garnish with fresh chopped dill or mint and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Mushrooms polenta
ingredients: 1 lb mixed mushrooms (such as cremini, shiitake, or oyster), sliced
1/4 cup vegetable broth or dry white wine
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 7-10 minutes.

Step 2: Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for another minute until fragrant. Pour in the vegetable broth or white wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly reduced.
Step 3: Stir in the chopped fresh parsley, salt, and pepper. Remove the mushroom mixture from the heat and set aside.
Step 4: In a separate medium saucepan, bring the 4 cups of vegetable broth or water to a boil over medium-high heat. Add a pinch of salt.

Step 5: Slowly whisk in the polenta in a steady stream to prevent lumps. Reduce the heat to low and continue to whisk frequently until the polenta thickens and pulls away from the sides of the pan, about 5-7 minutes for instant polenta or 15-20 minutes for quick-cooking polenta.
Step 6: Remove the polenta from the heat. Stir in the unsalted butter and grated Parmesan cheese (if using) until melted and well combined. Taste and adjust salt if needed.
Step 7: Ladle the hot polenta into bowls and top generously with the sautéed mushrooms. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Cinnamon sugar pop over
ingredients: 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted, plus more for greasing
steps: Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C). Place a popover pan or a 6-cup muffin tin in the oven to preheat for at least 5-10 minutes.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs until light and frothy. Gradually whisk in the milk and 2 tablespoons of melted butter until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Whisk until just combined and smooth, being careful not to overmix. A few small lumps are okay.
Carefully remove the hot popover pan from the oven. Grease each cup generously with butter.
Fill each cup about two-thirds full with the batter.
Return the pan to the oven and bake at 450°F (230°C) for 20 minutes. Do not open the oven door during this time.
Reduce the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue to bake for another 15-20 minutes, or until the popovers are deeply golden brown and puffed.
While the popovers are baking, combine the 1/4 cup granulated sugar and 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon in a small bowl to make the cinnamon sugar.
Remove the popovers from the oven. Immediately remove them from the pan and serve hot, sprinkled generously with the cinnamon sugar.

recipes.title: Chicken and orzo bake
ingredients: 500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper.

Step 2: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Step 3: Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 3-5 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and dried oregano, and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Pour in the diced tomatoes and chicken broth. Bring the mixture to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Step 5: Stir in the orzo pasta and return the cooked chicken to the skillet. Ensure the orzo is mostly submerged in the liquid. Bring back to a gentle simmer.

Step 6: Cover the skillet (or transfer to an oven-safe baking dish and cover with foil) and bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the orzo is tender and most of the liquid has been absorbed.
Step 7: Remove from the oven. Stir in the fresh spinach until it wilts. If using, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Step 8: Let stand for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired.

recipes.title: Protein wrap
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat 1 tsp olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Whisk 2 large eggs with salt and pepper. Pour into the hot pan and scramble until cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
Lay the whole wheat tortilla flat on a clean surface.
Spread the scrambled eggs over the center of the tortilla.
Top the eggs with fresh spinach, sliced avocado, shredded cheddar cheese (if using), and salsa.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla, then roll it up tightly from the bottom to form a wrap.
Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Pad Thai
ingredients: 8 oz (225g) raw shrimp, peeled and deveined, or chicken breast, thinly sliced
1/2 cup firm tofu, pressed and cut into small cubes (optional)
1/2 cup garlic chives, cut into 1-inch pieces, divided
1/4 cup roasted peanuts, crushed, for garnish
1 lime, cut into wedges, for serving
1 tsp red chili flakes (optional, for heat)
steps: Step 1: Soak the rice noodles in hot water for 20-30 minutes, or until pliable but still firm. Drain well and set aside.
Step 2: In a small bowl, whisk together the tamarind paste, fish sauce, palm sugar, and rice vinegar (if using) until the sugar is dissolved. This is your Pad Thai sauce.

Step 3: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the shrimp or chicken and cook until just pink or cooked through, about 2-3 minutes. Remove from the wok and set aside.
Step 4: Add another 1 tablespoon of oil to the wok. Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble until just set. Break them into smaller pieces and push to one side of the wok.
Step 5: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the empty side of the wok. Add the sliced shallots, minced garlic, and tofu (if using). Sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

Step 6: Add the drained noodles to the wok. Pour the Pad Thai sauce over the noodles and toss everything together, stirring constantly for 2-3 minutes, until the noodles are coated and softened. If the noodles are too dry, add a tablespoon or two of water.

Step 7: Return the cooked shrimp/chicken to the wok. Add 1.5 cups of the bean sprouts and most of the garlic chives (reserve some for garnish). Toss for another 1-2 minutes until the sprouts are slightly softened but still have a crunch.

Step 8: Transfer the Pad Thai to serving plates. Garnish with the remaining fresh bean sprouts, garlic chives, crushed peanuts, and a lime wedge. Serve immediately with chili flakes on the side, if desired.

recipes.title: One pot chicken adobe
ingredients: 2 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs or drumsticks
steps: Step 1: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Step 2: Brown chicken pieces on all sides until golden, about 3-5 minutes per side. Remove chicken from the pot and set aside.
Step 3: Reduce heat to medium. Add minced garlic to the pot and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Return chicken to the pot. Add soy sauce, vinegar, bay leaves, and whole black peppercorns. Add 1/2 cup water if you prefer more sauce.
Step 5: Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 30-40 minutes, or until chicken is tender and cooked through, stirring occasionally.
Step 6: Remove lid and increase heat to medium-high. Simmer for another 5-10 minutes, or until the sauce has reduced and thickened slightly to your desired consistency.
Step 7: Serve hot, traditionally with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Spaghetti with anchovies
ingredients: 6-8 anchovy fillets, packed in oil, drained
3 cloves garlic, thinly sliced or minced
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente. Before draining, reserve about 1 cup of the pasta cooking water.
While the spaghetti cooks, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat.
Add the sliced or minced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) to the skillet. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Add the drained anchovy fillets to the skillet. Cook, stirring and mashing the anchovies with the back of a spoon, until they dissolve into the oil, about 2-3 minutes.
Drain the spaghetti and add it directly to the skillet with the anchovy sauce.
Toss the spaghetti with the sauce, adding a few tablespoons of the reserved pasta water at a time to create a light, emulsified sauce that coats the pasta. Continue tossing for 1-2 minutes.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and lemon zest (if using).
Season with freshly ground black pepper and a pinch of salt if needed (anchovies are salty). Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Lemon braised chicken
ingredients: 2-3 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (thighs and drumsticks)
1 tbsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1 tsp dried)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Pat chicken pieces dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper.

Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium-high heat. Add chicken skin-side down and sear for 5-7 minutes until golden brown and crispy. Flip and sear for another 3-5 minutes. Remove chicken from the pot and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add chopped onion to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute more until fragrant.
Pour in white wine (if using) and chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Bring to a simmer.
Stir in fresh thyme and dried oregano. Add the slices from one lemon to the pot.
Return the seared chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the liquid. The liquid should come about halfway up the chicken.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer to a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C). Braise for 30-40 minutes, or until chicken is very tender and cooked through.
Remove from oven. Squeeze the juice from the remaining lemon over the chicken. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired, and serve hot.

recipes.title: One pan lemony chicken bake
ingredients: 2 pounds bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs3 tbsp olive oilSalt & pep1 shallot, minced3 tbsp fresh dill, finely chopped5 garlic cloves, minced1 tsp lemon zest2 tbsp Dijon mustard1/4cup grated pecorino2 lemons
2 tbsp parsley, chopped (for garnish)4 lemon wedges (for garnish)
steps: Preheat oven to 425F.
Pat the chicken dry and place in a baking dish.
Massage with 2 tbsp olive oil, season with salt + pepper.
Add shallot, dill, garlic, lemon zest, mustard, pecorino, and 4 tbsp lemon juice.
Rub everything into the chicken. Let sit for 30 mins to 2 hrs if possible.
Pour the remaining 2 tbsp lemon juice over the top + drizzle with the last tbsp of olive oil.
Bake for 30-35 mins. Baste with pan juices, then bake 10-15 more until golden and cooked through.
Spoon the drippings over the top, garnish with parsley + lemon wedges, and serve!

recipes.title: Creamy chicken lemon rice soup
ingredients: 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts or thighs, cooked and shredded or diced
1 cup uncooked long-grain white rice (or Arborio rice)
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the cooked and shredded chicken and cook for 2-3 minutes until lightly browned. Remove chicken from the pot and set aside.
Step 2: Add butter to the same pot. Once melted, add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are softened.
Step 3: Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Step 4: Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir constantly for 1-2 minutes to create a roux.
Step 5: Gradually whisk in the chicken broth, ensuring no lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer, stirring occasionally.
Step 6: Add the uncooked rice to the pot. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Step 7: Stir in the reserved cooked chicken, heavy cream, and fresh lemon juice. Heat through for 2-3 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream.
Step 8: Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh chopped parsley before serving.

recipes.title: Harissa chicken thighs
steps: orange juiced 14 cup low fat Greek yogurt
tbsp parsley
bone-in chicken thighs, skin removed
Preheat oven to 375F
To a medium sized bowl, add Harissa, honey, olive oil, salt, lemon juice, orange juice, parsley and Greek yogurt to a bowl. Whisk until combine.
Add the chicken to the bowl with the marinade and toss to coat. Cover with plastic wrap and store in the fridge for at least 1 hour and up to 10 hours.
Serve warm with rice or roasted veggies or both!

recipes.title: Apple tart
ingredients: 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, very cold and cubed
6-8 medium apples (e.g., Granny Smith, Honeycrisp), peeled, cored, and thinly sliced
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour (for filling, or cornstarch)
2 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
steps: Step 1: Prepare the pie crust. In a large bowl, whisk together 2 cups flour, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 cup granulated sugar. Cut in the cold, cubed butter using a pastry blender or your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs with some pea-sized pieces of butter remaining. Gradually add ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time, mixing until the dough just comes together. Do not overmix. Divide the dough in half, flatten each half into a disc, wrap in plastic, and chill for at least 30 minutes.

Step 2: Prepare the apple filling. In a large bowl, combine the sliced apples, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, lemon juice, and 2 tablespoons flour. Toss gently to coat all the apple slices evenly.

Step 3: Preheat oven and assemble. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). On a lightly floured surface, roll out one disc of chilled dough into a 12-inch circle. Carefully transfer the dough to a 9-inch tart pan or pie plate. Trim the edges and crimp as desired. Pour the apple filling into the crust, mounding it slightly in the center. Dot the top of the filling with the small pieces of butter.

Step 4: Add top crust. Roll out the second disc of dough. You can either place it whole over the filling (cutting several slits for steam to escape) or cut it into strips to create a lattice top. Place the top crust over the filling and seal the edges with the bottom crust. If desired, brush the top with an egg wash (1 egg yolk beaten with 1 tbsp milk) and sprinkle with a little extra sugar.

Step 5: Bake the tart. Bake for 15 minutes at 400°F (200°C). Reduce the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue to bake for another 35-45 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the apples are tender when pierced with a knife. If the crust edges start to brown too quickly, cover them loosely with aluminum foil.
Step 6: Cool and serve. Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for at least 30 minutes before slicing and serving. This allows the filling to set properly.

recipes.title: Seared miso chicken with ginger broth
ingredients: 2-inch piece fresh ginger, thinly sliced (for broth)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: In a medium bowl, combine the miso paste, soy sauce, mirin, 1 tablespoon grated ginger, and 1 minced garlic clove. Add the chicken thighs and toss to coat evenly. Let marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature.

Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken. Sear for 5-7 minutes per side, until golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F/74°C). Remove chicken from the pan and set aside to rest.
While the chicken rests, add the 2-inch sliced ginger and 2 smashed garlic cloves to the same skillet (no need to clean). Sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let simmer for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. Season the broth with salt and pepper to taste.
Slice the rested chicken into ½-inch thick pieces.
To serve, divide the sliced chicken among bowls. Ladle the hot ginger broth over the chicken. Drizzle with a touch of sesame oil if desired, and garnish with thinly sliced scallions.

recipes.title: Mini berry crumb cake
ingredients: 1/4 cup packed light brown sugar (for crumb topping)
1/4 cup (4 tablespoons) cold unsalted butter, cut into small pieces (for crumb topping)
1/4 cup (4 tablespoons) unsalted butter, melted and cooled
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners or grease well.

**Make the Crumb Topping:** In a medium bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup flour, brown sugar, 2 tablespoons granulated sugar, and cinnamon. Add the cold butter pieces and use your fingertips or a pastry blender to cut the butter into the dry ingredients until coarse crumbs form. Set aside.
**Prepare the Berry Filling:** In a small bowl, gently toss the mixed berries with 2 tablespoons granulated sugar, cornstarch, and lemon juice (if using). Set aside.
**Make the Cake Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together 1 1/2 cups flour, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the egg, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a spatula until just combined. Do not overmix; a few lumps are okay.

**Assemble the Cakes:** Divide about half of the cake batter evenly among the prepared muffin cups. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the berry filling over the batter in each cup. Top with the remaining cake batter, then spoon the rest of the berry filling on top.
Generously sprinkle the reserved crumb topping over each cake.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center of a cake (avoiding a berry) comes out clean and the crumbs are golden brown.
Let the mini crumb cakes cool in the muffin tin for 5-10 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.

recipes.title: High protein pancakes
ingredients: 1 scoop (approx. 30g) vanilla or unflavored protein powder
1/2 cup oat flour (or whole wheat flour)
1 tbsp maple syrup or other sweetener (optional)
1 tsp oil or butter for cooking
steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, whisk together the protein powder, oat flour, baking powder, and salt.
Step 2: In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the egg, milk, and maple syrup (if using).
Step 3: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are okay.
Step 4: Heat a lightly oiled griddle or non-stick pan over medium heat.
Step 5: Pour about 1/4 cup of batter per pancake onto the hot griddle.
Step 6: Cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and bubbles form on the surface before flipping.
Step 7: Serve immediately with your favorite toppings.

recipes.title: Chicken sweet corn soup
ingredients: 5 whole corn - kernels extracted (or 800g frozen/canned kernels)
steps: 5 litres water
tbsp oil
eggs - whisked
tbsp cornflour mixed with 3 tbsp water for the thickening slurry
tsp chicken boullion powder

tsp salt white pepper to taste In a pot, fry the onion and garlic with oil for 1 min on high heat. Add half the corn kernels and water. Bring to a simmer and add seasoning, taste and turn off the heat. Stick blend the base soup. On medium heat add in the chicken and remaining corn kernels, bring to a simmer and mix in the thickening slurry. Carefully drizzle the egg into the mixture, stir, turn off the heat and its ready to serve! Garnish with optional spring onion greens and perfect accompanied with rice.

recipes.title: Charcuterie sliders
ingredients: 1 brie wheel, sliced WHEN COLD TO MAKE IT EASIER
steps: Halve bread rolls and remove top.
Dress arugula with olive oil and flakey salt.

recipes.title: Spaghetti garlic anchovies
steps: Step 1: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente.

Step 2: While the spaghetti cooks, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add the sliced garlic and cook gently until fragrant and lightly golden, about 2-3 minutes. Be careful not to burn the garlic.

Step 3: Add the drained anchovy fillets to the skillet. Using the back of a spoon, mash the anchovies into the oil until they dissolve and create a paste. Add the red pepper flakes, if using, and cook for another minute.
Step 4: Before draining the spaghetti, reserve about 1 cup of the starchy pasta water. Drain the spaghetti and add it directly to the skillet with the garlic and anchovy sauce.
Step 5: Toss the spaghetti with the sauce, adding a few tablespoons of the reserved pasta water at a time, until the sauce emulsifies and coats the pasta nicely. Continue tossing for 1-2 minutes.
Step 6: Remove from heat, stir in the fresh chopped parsley, and season with black pepper to taste. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Shrimp, tofu and mushrooms soup
ingredients: 1 tbsp neutral oil (like canola or vegetable)
1 block (14 oz) firm or extra-firm tofu, drained and cubed
1 lb medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat the oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Step 2: Add the sliced mushrooms to the pot and sauté for 3-5 minutes until they soften and release their liquid.
Step 3: Pour in the chicken or vegetable broth. Stir in the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil. Bring the soup to a gentle simmer.
Step 4: Gently add the cubed tofu and chopped bok choy to the simmering soup. Cook for 3-5 minutes, or until the bok choy is tender-crisp.

Step 5: If desired, in a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and cold water to create a slurry. Stir the slurry into the simmering soup and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the soup slightly thickens.
Step 6: Add the peeled and deveined shrimp to the soup. Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the shrimp turn pink and opaque. Be careful not to overcook the shrimp.
Step 7: Taste the soup and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed. Ladle the hot soup into bowls and garnish with sliced green onions before serving.

recipes.title: Clam pasta
ingredients: 2 pounds fresh littleneck clams, scrubbed and purged
1/2 cup dry white wine or chicken broth
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipes.notes: Ingredients not detected automatically.
recipes.descriptionPreview: This quick and easy clam pasta recipe is a game-changer for busy weeknights. With just a few ingredients and a simple cooking process, you can enjoy a flavorful and satisfying meal in no time. Learn how to make it with our step-by-step guide and get ready to elevate your pasta ga
steps: Step 1: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the linguine and cook according to package directions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.

Step 2: While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Step 3: Add the scrubbed clams and white wine (or broth) to the skillet. Cover tightly and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the clams have opened. Discard any clams that do not open.

Step 4: Remove the clams from the skillet and set aside. Add the drained pasta to the skillet with the clam juice. Add the chopped parsley and butter (if using). Toss to combine, adding a splash of the reserved pasta water if needed to create a light sauce.
Step 5: Return the clams to the skillet with the pasta. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Toss gently to combine and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Asparagus omellete
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp unsalted butter or olive oil
1 tbsp shredded cheese (cheddar, feta, or goat cheese, optional)
steps: Step 1: Trim the woody ends off the asparagus spears. Chop the tender parts into 1/2-inch pieces.
Step 2: In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with milk (if using), salt, and pepper until well combined and slightly frothy.
Step 3: Heat butter or olive oil in an 8-inch non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped asparagus and sauté for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender-crisp.
Step 4: Pour the egg mixture into the skillet over the asparagus. Let it cook undisturbed for about 1-2 minutes, until the edges begin to set.

Step 5: Gently push the cooked edges towards the center with a spatula, tilting the pan to allow uncooked egg to flow underneath. Repeat until most of the egg is set but the top is still slightly moist.
Step 6: If using cheese, sprinkle it over one half of the omelette.
Step 7: Carefully fold the omelette in half using the spatula. Cook for another 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until cooked to your desired doneness.
Step 8: Slide the asparagus omelette onto a plate and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Eggplant tomatoes and mozzarella
ingredients: 3 tbsp olive oil, divided, plus more for drizzling
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
1/4 cup fresh basil leaves, torn or chopped, plus more for garnish
steps: Slice the eggplant into 1/2-inch thick rounds. Lay them on paper towels, sprinkle generously with salt, and let sit for 30 minutes to draw out moisture. Pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).

Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

Stir in the crushed tomatoes and dried oregano. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and let the sauce cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

While the sauce simmers, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a separate large skillet over medium-high heat. Working in batches, pan-fry the eggplant slices for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and tender. Add more oil as needed.

Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce on the bottom of an 8x8 inch or similar baking dish. Arrange a layer of cooked eggplant slices over the sauce. Top with a layer of mozzarella slices, a sprinkle of Parmesan cheese (if using), and some fresh basil.
Repeat the layers: sauce, eggplant, mozzarella, Parmesan, and basil, until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of sauce and mozzarella on top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the dish is heated through.
Let rest for 5-10 minutes before serving. Garnish with extra fresh basil.

recipes.title: Onion and egg mushroom omelette
steps: Step 1: Heat olive oil or butter in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.

Step 2: Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook for another 3-5 minutes, or until they are tender and have released their moisture. Remove the cooked vegetables from the skillet and set aside.
Step 3: In a medium bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk (if using), salt, and pepper until well combined and slightly frothy.
Step 4: Return the skillet to medium heat (add a tiny bit more oil if needed). Pour the egg mixture into the hot skillet, tilting the pan to evenly coat the bottom.
Step 5: As the edges of the omelette begin to set, gently push the cooked egg towards the center with a spatula, allowing the uncooked egg to flow underneath.
Step 6: When the omelette is mostly set but still slightly moist on top, sprinkle the cooked onion and mushroom mixture over one half of the omelette.
Step 7: Carefully fold the other half of the omelette over the filling. Cook for another 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until heated through.
Step 8: Slide the omelette onto a plate and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Tomatoes and ricotta tart
steps: Step 1: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Step 2: Unroll the puff pastry onto the prepared baking sheet. Score a 1-inch border around the edges of the pastry with a sharp knife, being careful not to cut all the way through.
Step 3: In a medium bowl, combine the ricotta cheese, eggs, Parmesan cheese, chopped basil, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Step 4: Spread the ricotta mixture evenly within the scored border of the puff pastry.
Step 5: Arrange the sliced tomatoes on top of the ricotta mixture. Drizzle lightly with olive oil.
Step 6: Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed, and the filling is set.
Step 7: Let the tart cool slightly on the baking sheet before slicing and serving.

recipes.title: Spanish omelette
ingredients: 4-5 medium potatoes (about 700g), peeled and thinly sliced
1/2 cup olive oil (plus more for cooking)
1/4 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
steps: Step 1: Heat 1/2 cup olive oil in a large, non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced potatoes and onion (if using). Season with salt and pepper.

Step 2: Cook the potatoes and onion, stirring occasionally, until they are very tender and slightly golden, about 15-20 minutes. Do not let them brown too much. Drain the potatoes and onion in a colander, reserving about 1 tablespoon of the olive oil in the skillet.
Step 3: While the potatoes are draining, crack the eggs into a large bowl. Whisk them vigorously with a fork until well combined. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Step 4: Add the drained potatoes and onion to the whisked eggs. Stir gently to combine, ensuring all the potatoes are coated with egg.

Step 5: Return the skillet with the reserved olive oil to medium-low heat. Pour the egg and potato mixture into the skillet, spreading it evenly. Cook for about 5-7 minutes, or until the edges are set and golden, and the bottom is firm.

Step 6: To flip the omelette, place a large plate (larger than the skillet) over the skillet. Carefully invert the skillet onto the plate, so the omelette is now on the plate, cooked side up. Slide the omelette back into the skillet, uncooked side down.
Step 7: Continue to cook for another 5-7 minutes, or until the omelette is firm in the center and cooked through. The outside should be golden brown.
Step 8: Slide the Spanish omelette onto a clean plate. Let it cool slightly before slicing into wedges and serving warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Leek mushroom quiche
ingredients: 2 medium leeks, white and light green parts only, thinly sliced
recipes.notes: Ingredients not detected automatically.
recipes.descriptionPreview: Discover a game-changing quiche recipe that combines a crispy potato crust with a hearty leek, mushroom, and spinach filling. This brunch or dinner idea is perfect for a cozy evening or a special occasion.
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). If using a homemade crust, blind bake it according to recipe instructions, or if using a store-bought crust, prepare it as directed (some require pre-baking, some do not).

Step 2: In a large skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add sliced leeks and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add sliced mushrooms and continue to cook until mushrooms are tender and any liquid has evaporated, about 8-10 minutes. Remove from heat.
Step 3: Sprinkle half of the shredded cheese evenly over the bottom of the pie crust. Spread the cooked leek and mushroom mixture over the cheese.
Step 4: In a medium bowl, whisk together eggs, half-and-half, salt, pepper, and nutmeg until well combined.
Step 5: Carefully pour the egg mixture over the leek and mushroom filling in the pie crust. Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top.
Step 6: Bake for 40-50 minutes, or until the center is set and a knife inserted near the center comes out clean. The top should be golden brown.
Step 7: Let the quiche cool on a wire rack for at least 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.

recipes.title: Stacked eggplant
ingredients: 1/4 cup olive oil, plus more for baking sheet
1/2 teaspoon salt, plus more for eggplant
8 ounces fresh mozzarella cheese, sliced or shredded
1/4 cup fresh basil leaves, chopped, plus more for garnish
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Slice eggplants into 1/2-inch thick rounds. Lay slices on paper towels, sprinkle generously with salt, and let sit for 30 minutes to draw out moisture. Pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.

Step 2: Brush eggplant slices lightly with olive oil on both sides. Arrange in a single layer on a baking sheet. Bake for 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, until tender and lightly golden. Remove from oven and set aside.

Step 3: While eggplant bakes, prepare the tomato sauce. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in crushed tomatoes, oregano, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened. Stir in the chopped fresh basil.

Step 4: Reduce oven temperature to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a baking sheet or line with parchment paper. To assemble the stacks, place an eggplant slice on the baking sheet. Top with a spoonful of tomato sauce, a slice or sprinkle of mozzarella, and a pinch of Parmesan cheese. Repeat with another eggplant slice, sauce, and cheeses. Finish with a third eggplant slice, more sauce, and a generous layer of mozzarella and Parmesan on top.
Step 5: Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the stacks are heated through. If desired, broil for the last 1-2 minutes to achieve a golden-brown crust on the cheese.
Step 6: Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve warm.

recipes.title: Mustard chicken
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh thyme leaves, chopped (or 1 tsp dried)
1/4 cup heavy cream (optional, for a richer sauce)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
